dialogues.title: Planning to meet at the market
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Maria and João, are chatting outside their homes in Funchal.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Olá, Maria! Que tal ir ao mercado amanhã de manhã?
lines.translation: Hi, Maria! How about going to the market tomorrow morning?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Ah, João! Boa ideia! Preciso de fruta fresca.
lines.translation: Ah, João! Good idea! I need fresh fruit.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Pois. Vamos juntos às nove e meia?
lines.translation: Right. Shall we go together at half past nine?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Às nove e meia? Sim, combinado! Encontramo-nos à entrada.
lines.translation: At half past nine? Yes, agreed! We'll meet at the entrance.

dialogues.title: Suggesting a coffee break
dialogues.context: Two colleagues, Ana and Pedro, are finishing work in a Funchal office.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Uff, finalmente! Que tal ir beber uma bica antes de ir p'ra casa?
lines.translation: Ugh, finally! How about going for an espresso before going home?

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Uma bica? Boa ideia, Ana! Estou com sede.
lines.translation: An espresso? Good idea, Ana! I'm thirsty.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Vamos ao café ali na rua, então?
lines.translation: Shall we go to the café down the street, then?

lines.speaker: Pedro
lines.text: Sim, combinado! Tanto faz o café, desde que tenha bica.
lines.translation: Yes, agreed! Any café is fine, as long as it has espresso.

roleplay.scenario: You want to make plans with a friend to go to the beach or a levada walk. Your friend suggests a time, and you need to agree or suggest an alternative.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Que tal ir à praia no sábado?
nodes.npc_translation: Hi! How about going to the beach on Saturday?

options.text: Boa ideia!
options.translation: Good idea!
options.feedback: You agreed with the suggestion. Now you need to discuss the time.

options.text: Hum, à praia? Não sei. Que tal ir à levada?
options.translation: Hmm, to the beach? I don't know. How about going to the levada?
options.feedback: You suggested an alternative activity. Good negotiation!

nodes.npc_text: Ótimo! Às dez da manhã, combinado?
nodes.npc_translation: Great! At ten in the morning, agreed?

options.text: Sim, combinado!
options.translation: Yes, agreed!
options.feedback: Perfect! You've made plans.

options.text: Às dez? Não posso. Que tal às onze?
options.translation: At ten? I can't. How about at eleven?
options.feedback: You couldn't make that time and suggested an alternative. Well done!

nodes.npc_text: À levada? Boa ideia também! Gosto de caminhar. A que horas?
nodes.npc_translation: To the levada? Good idea too! I like walking. At what time?

options.text: Que tal às nove?
options.translation: How about at nine?
options.feedback: You suggested a time for the alternative activity.

options.text: Tanto faz, tu escolhes.
options.translation: It doesn't matter, you choose.
options.feedback: You left the choice to your friend, using a known phrase.

nodes.npc_text: Às onze? Sim, pode ser. Combinado!
nodes.npc_translation: At eleven? Yes, that works. Agreed!

options.text: Até sábado, então!
options.translation: See you Saturday, then!
options.feedback: You confirmed the plan. Excellent!

nodes.npc_text: Às nove? Sim, combinado. Encontramo-nos no início da levada.
nodes.npc_translation: At nine? Yes, agreed. We'll meet at the start of the levada.

options.text: Perfeito! Até lá.
options.translation: Perfect! See you then.
options.feedback: You've successfully made plans for the levada walk.

nodes.npc_text: Então, às dez e meia? É bom p'ra mim.
nodes.npc_translation: So, at half past ten? That's good for me.

options.text: Sim, combinado!
options.translation: Yes, agreed!
options.feedback: You agreed to your friend's chosen time. Good job!

mission.title: Plan a Coffee Break with a Colleague
prep: Think about a good time to suggest for a coffee break.
Practice suggesting 'Que tal ir beber uma bica?'
Be ready to agree with 'Combinado!' or suggest an alternative time if needed.

cultural_notes.title: The Importance of 'Bica'
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, 'bica' is the most common way to order an espresso coffee. It's a daily ritual for many, often enjoyed quickly at the counter of a 'café' (coffee shop). Suggesting 'ir beber uma bica' is a very common and friendly way to invite someone for a quick social break.

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Refusal
cultural_notes.body: When you need to refuse a suggestion or an invitation, it's polite to offer a brief reason or an alternative, rather than a direct 'Não'. Phrases like 'Não posso, mas que tal...' (I can't, but how about...) or 'Não posso a essa hora' (I can't at that time) are common and soften the refusal.

dialogues.title: At the Café
dialogues.context: A customer is ordering breakfast at a typical Madeiran café.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Bom dia! Diga, o que deseja?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. Queria uma bica e um bolo do caco, por favor.
lines.translation: Good morning. I'd like an espresso and a bolo do caco, please.

lines.speaker: Empregado

lines.translation: An espresso and a bolo do caco. Anything else?

lines.speaker: Cliente

dialogues.title: At the Market Stall
dialogues.context: A customer is buying vegetables at a market stall in Funchal.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Olá! Posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Hello! Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Olá. Tem semilhas? Queria um quilo, por favor.
lines.translation: Hello. Do you have potatoes? I'd like a kilo, please.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Tenho sim. Aqui está. Mais alguma coisa?
lines.translation: Yes, I do. Here you go. Anything else?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Não, só as semilhas. Quanto custa?
lines.translation: No, just the potatoes. How much is it?

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Fica a um euro e cinquenta. Obrigada!

roleplay.scenario: You are at a fruit stall in a local market in Madeira, buying some fruit.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! O que vai ser hoje?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! What will it be today?

options.text: Bom dia. Queria umas bananas, por favor.
options.translation: Good morning. I'd like some bananas, please.
options.feedback: A good start, clearly stating what you want.

options.text: Olá. O que é que tem de bom?
options.translation: Hello. What do you have that's good?
options.feedback: This is a polite way to ask for recommendations.

nodes.npc_text: Bananas, claro! Quantas quer?
nodes.npc_translation: Bananas, of course! How many do you want?

options.text: Umas cinco, se faz favor.
options.translation: About five, please.
options.feedback: Specify the quantity clearly.

options.text: Um quilo, por favor.
options.translation: A kilo, please.
options.feedback: Using 'quilo' is common for fruit.

nodes.npc_text: Temos umas anonas ótimas hoje, e as peras também estão muito boas!
nodes.npc_translation: We have some great custard apples today, and the pears are also very good!

options.text: Ah, sim? Queria umas anonas então.
options.translation: Oh, really? I'd like some custard apples then.
options.feedback: Accepting the recommendation is natural.

options.text: Obrigado, mas queria umas maçãs, por favor.
options.translation: Thank you, but I'd like some apples, please.
options.feedback: It's fine to politely decline and ask for something else.

nodes.npc_text: Quantas anonas?
nodes.npc_translation: How many custard apples?

options.text: Duas, por favor.
options.translation: Two, please.
options.feedback: State the quantity.

nodes.npc_text: Maçãs, pois. Quantas?
nodes.npc_translation: Apples, right. How many?

options.text: Três, por favor.
options.translation: Three, please.
options.feedback: State the quantity.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Mais alguma coisa?
nodes.npc_translation: Right. Anything else?

options.text: Não, é tudo. Quanto é?
options.translation: No, that's all. How much is it?
options.feedback: Time to ask for the total.

options.text: Sim, queria também um quilo de laranjas.
options.translation: Yes, I'd also like a kilo of oranges.
options.feedback: You can add more items to your purchase.

nodes.npc_text: Então, fica a cinco euros e dez cêntimos. Obrigado!
nodes.npc_translation: So, it's five euros and ten cents. Thank you!

options.text: Aqui tem. Obrigada!
options.translation: Here you go. Thank you!
options.feedback: A polite way to complete the transaction.

mission.title: Order Your Coffee and Cake
prep: Practice ordering a 'bica' and a 'bolo do caco'.
Rehearse asking 'Quanto é?' or 'Quanto custa?' for the price.
Prepare to say 'Não, obrigada' if asked 'Mais alguma coisa?'.
likely_responses: São X euros e Y cêntimos.

cultural_notes.title: Bica vs. Café
cultural_notes.body: In mainland Portugal and Madeira, the most common way to order an espresso is to ask for a 'bica'. While 'café' is understood, 'bica' is the local and more natural term. It's a small, strong coffee, usually served quickly at the counter.

cultural_notes.title: The Versatile 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' is a very common and versatile interjection in Portuguese. It can mean 'yes', 'indeed', 'of course', or simply acknowledge what someone has said. It's often used to express agreement or confirmation, similar to 'right' or 'exactly' in English. For example, 'Pois é!' means 'That's right!' or 'Indeed!'

dialogues.title: Recent Activities

lines.speaker: Chefe

roleplay.scenario: You're talking to a local Madeiran in a café. They ask what you've done or haven't done yet on the island.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Então, já tens gostado da Madeira? Tens visto muitas coisas?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! So, have you liked Madeira? Have you seen many things?

options.text: Sim, tenho adorado! Tenho visto sítios incríveis.
options.translation: Yes, I've loved it! I've seen incredible places.
options.feedback: Great start! You've used 'adorado' and 'visto' correctly.

options.text: Tenho gostado, mas ainda não tenho visto muito.
options.translation: I've liked it, but I still haven't seen much.
options.feedback: Good use of 'ainda não tenho visto' to express something not yet done.

nodes.npc_text: Que bom! E já tens provado a poncha, ou ainda não?
nodes.npc_translation: That's good! And have you already tried poncha, or not yet?

options.text: Sim, já tenho provado! É muito boa.
options.translation: Yes, I've already tried it! It's very good.
options.feedback: Excellent! You used 'já tenho provado' for a completed action.

options.text: Ainda não tenho provado a poncha. Quero provar!
options.translation: I still haven't tried poncha. I want to try it!
options.feedback: Perfect use of 'ainda não tenho provado' and expressing desire.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, entendo. E tens feito alguma levada? Ou ainda não tens tido tempo?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, I understand. And have you done any levada? Or haven't you had time yet?

options.text: Ainda não tenho feito nenhuma. Tenho estado ocupado.
options.translation: I still haven't done any. I've been busy.
options.feedback: Good, you've explained why you haven't done it using 'tenho estado'.

options.text: Sim, já tenho feito uma! Foi lindo.
options.translation: Yes, I've already done one! It was beautiful.
options.feedback: Well done! You used 'já tenho feito' for a completed action.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, a poncha é famosa! E já tens visitado o Mercado dos Lavradores?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, poncha is famous! And have you already visited the Farmers' Market?

options.text: Sim, já tenho visitado. Tenho comprado muitas frutas.
options.translation: Yes, I've already visited. I've bought many fruits.
options.feedback: Fantastic! You used 'já tenho visitado' and 'tenho comprado' correctly.

options.text: Ainda não tenho ido, mas quero ir amanhã.
options.translation: I still haven't gone, but I want to go tomorrow.
options.feedback: Excellent, 'ainda não tenho ido' is perfect here, and you added a future plan.

mission.title: Describe Your Recent Experiences in Madeira
prep: Think about 2-3 things you have done recently in Madeira.
Think about 1-2 things you haven't done yet but want to.
Practice saying 'Tenho adorado estar cá.'
Practice saying 'Tenho visto coisas incríveis.'
Practice saying 'Ainda não tenho ido ao...' or 'Ainda não tenho feito...'

cultural_notes.title: The Versatility of 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'Pois é' are very common interjections in Portugal, especially in informal conversation. They can mean 'yes, that's right', 'indeed', 'of course', or simply acknowledge what the other person has said, showing active listening. It's often used to agree or confirm a statement, much like 'right' or 'exactly' in English.

cultural_notes.title: Directness in Conversation
cultural_notes.body: Portuguese communication, especially in Madeira, tends to be quite direct. While politeness is valued, people are generally straightforward in their questions and statements. Don't be surprised by direct questions about your plans or experiences; it's usually a sign of friendly interest, not rudeness.

dialogues.title: Daily Routine Chat
dialogues.context: Two friends, Ana and Carlos, are talking over coffee in a café.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Então, Carlos, já te habituaste aos horários daqui?
lines.translation: So, Carlos, have you already gotten used to the schedules here?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Sim, já me sinto em casa! Mas às vezes esqueço-me de algumas coisas, como as horas dos autocarros.
lines.translation: Yes, I already feel at home! But sometimes I forget some things, like the bus times.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Pois é, é preciso lembrar-se bem! Eu levanto-me sempre cedo para não me atrasar.
lines.translation: Indeed, you really need to remember! I always get up early so I don't get late.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Eu também me levanto cedo, mas ontem levantei-me um bocado tarde. Não te preocupes, hoje não me esqueço!
lines.translation: I also get up early, but yesterday I got up a bit late. Don't worry, today I won't forget!

dialogues.title: Feeling Settled
dialogues.context: A tourist, Maria, is talking to a hotel receptionist, João, on the phone.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Bom dia, Dona Maria. Como se sente hoje?
lines.translation: Good morning, Ms. Maria. How are you feeling today?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Bom dia, João. Sinto-me muito bem, obrigada! Já me habituei ao fuso horário.
lines.translation: Good morning, João. I feel very well, thank you! I've already gotten used to the time zone.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Que bom! Não se esqueça da excursão às levadas hoje à tarde.
lines.translation: That's good! Don't forget about the levada excursion this afternoon.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Não me esqueço! Já me lembro de tudo. Obrigada!
lines.translation: I won't forget! I already remember everything. Thank you!

roleplay.scenario: You're chatting with a local Madeiran neighbour about daily life and getting settled in. They are friendly and curious.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Já te sentes bem aqui na Madeira?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! Do you already feel good here in Madeira?

options.text: Sim, já me sinto em casa, obrigada!
options.translation: Yes, I already feel at home, thank you!
options.feedback: Great start, using 'sentir-se' correctly!

options.text: Ainda não me sinto muito bem.
options.translation: I still don't feel very good.
options.feedback: Good, you used 'sentir-se' in the negative. Let's see what the neighbour says.

nodes.npc_text: Percebo. E já te habituaste ao ritmo da ilha?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. And have you already gotten used to the island's pace?

options.text: Sim, já me habituei, é mais calmo.
options.translation: Yes, I've already gotten used to it, it's calmer.
options.feedback: Excellent use of 'habituar-se' in the past tense!

options.text: Ainda me estou a habituar, é um bocado diferente.
options.translation: I'm still getting used to it, it's a bit different.
options.feedback: Good, 'estar a habituar-se' for ongoing action.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é. E lembras-te de levar sempre um casaco? O tempo muda rápido!
nodes.npc_translation: Indeed. And do you remember to always take a jacket? The weather changes quickly!

options.text: Sim, lembro-me sempre! É importante.
options.translation: Yes, I always remember! It's important.
options.feedback: Perfect! 'Lembrar-se' in the present.

options.text: Às vezes esqueço-me, mas tento lembrar-me.
options.translation: Sometimes I forget, but I try to remember.
options.feedback: Good, you used both 'esquecer-se' and 'lembrar-se'!

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem! Fico feliz por te sentires bem aqui. Se precisares de algo, não te preocupes em perguntar.
nodes.npc_translation: Very good! I'm happy you feel good here. If you need anything, don't worry about asking.

nodes.npc_text: Não te preocupes, é normal no início. Vais habituar-te!
nodes.npc_translation: Don't worry, it's normal at first. You'll get used to it!

mission.title: Chatting About Your Day
prep: Practice describing your morning routine using reflexive verbs: 'Levanto-me às...', 'Visto-me...', 'Lavo-me...'.
Think about something you often forget or remember to do.
Be ready to ask someone about their routine or how they are feeling.
fallback_phrases: Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?

cultural_notes.title: The Versatility of 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'pois é' are very common interjections in Portuguese. They can mean 'yes', 'indeed', 'of course', or simply acknowledge what someone said. It's often used to show agreement or understanding in a conversation, much like 'right' or 'yeah' in English. It can also be used to prompt someone to continue speaking.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing People: 'Tu' vs. 'O Senhor/A Senhora'
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, the informal 'tu' is generally used with friends, family, and people your own age or younger. For people older than you, in service situations, or with strangers you want to show respect to, use 'o senhor' (for a man) or 'a senhora' (for a woman) with the third person verb form. Using 'você' can sometimes sound a bit distant or even impolite, so it's best to avoid it as a default polite form.

review_items.prompt: Say this phrase from memory: "Levanto-me às sete."
Say this phrase from memory: "Já me sinto em casa."
Say this phrase from memory: "Não te preocupes."

Recall the Portuguese for: to forget

situations.title: Emotions, Empathy & Comfort
situations.summary: Express and respond to emotional states naturally.

variants.text: Ele está com saudade da família.

dialogues.title: Missing a Friend
dialogues.context: Two friends, Ana and Marco, are talking in a café about a friend who moved away.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Então, o João já foi para Lisboa, não foi?
lines.translation: So, João already went to Lisbon, didn't he?

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Pois é, foi ontem. Já estou com saudade dele, sabes?
lines.translation: Yes, he went yesterday. I already miss him, you know?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Ai, que pena! Ficamos todos um bocado tristes, pois é.
lines.translation: Oh, what a pity! We all feel a bit sad, don't we.

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Sim, mas ele estava feliz por ir. Eu estou emocionado por ele.
lines.translation: Yes, but he was happy to go. I'm emotional for him.

roleplay.scenario: Your friend, Marta, tells you she's sad because her pet is sick.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Não estou muito feliz hoje, sabes? O meu gato está doente.
nodes.npc_translation: Hi! I'm not very happy today, you know? My cat is sick.

options.text: Ai, que pena! Sinto muito.
options.translation: Oh, what a pity! I'm so sorry.
options.feedback: A good empathetic start.

options.text: Ah, não digas isso! O que se passa?
options.translation: Oh, don't say that! What's happening?
options.feedback: A natural way to ask for more information.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é. Levei-o ao veterinário, mas ele está um bocado triste e não come.
nodes.npc_translation: That's it. I took him to the vet, but he's a bit sad and not eating.

options.text: Fiquei sem palavras! Espero que ele melhore.
options.translation: I'm speechless! I hope he gets better.
options.feedback: Expresses strong empathy and hope.

options.text: Estou com saudade de o ver bem. É mesmo triste.
options.translation: I miss seeing him well. It's really sad.
options.feedback: Shows you understand her emotional connection to the pet.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada por ouvires. É bom falar contigo.
nodes.npc_translation: Thanks for listening. It's good to talk to you.

options.text: De nada. Se precisares de alguma coisa, diz.
options.translation: You're welcome. If you need anything, just say.
options.feedback: Offers practical support and ends the conversation positively.

options.text: Não faz mal. Espero que tudo corra bem.
options.translation: It's nothing. I hope everything goes well.
options.feedback: Reassures her and offers a simple wish.

mission.title: Comfort a Friend
prep: Listen carefully to what your friend is saying.
Use 'Que pena!' or 'Sinto muito' to show empathy.
If appropriate, use 'Estou com saudade' to express missing something/someone, or 'triste' to acknowledge sadness.

cultural_notes.title: The unique meaning of 'Saudade'
cultural_notes.body: The word 'saudade' is often considered untranslatable and embodies a deep emotional state of nostalgic longing for something or someone absent. It can refer to a person, a place, a moment, or even a feeling. It implies that the object of longing may never return, or that the joy of reunion is uncertain. It's a very cherished and understood emotion in Portuguese culture.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Empathy Directly
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, it's common and appreciated to express empathy directly when someone shares a difficult feeling or situation. Phrases like 'Que pena!' (What a pity!) or 'Sinto muito' (I'm very sorry) are common and show you are listening and care. Don't be afraid to acknowledge sadness or difficulty.

Say this phrase from memory: "Fiquei sem palavras!"

situations.title: Madeiran Geography & Culture
situations.summary: Speak about Madeira with depth and specificity.

phrase_patterns.base: É uma ilha única no mundo.

variants.text: É uma ilha muito bonita, sim.
variants.translation: It's a very beautiful island, yes.

variants.text: É uma ilha especial no Atlântico.
variants.translation: It's a special island in the Atlantic.

variants.text: É uma ilha fantástica, de facto.

phrase_patterns.base: A paisagem é de cortar a respiração.

variants.text: A vista é de cortar a respiração, não é?
variants.translation: The view is breathtaking, isn't it?

variants.text: As levadas percorrem quase toda a ilha.
variants.translation: The levadas go through almost the entire island.

variants.text: As levadas passam por muitas zonas da ilha.
variants.translation: The levadas pass through many areas of the island.

variants.text: Sim, as levadas atravessam a ilha toda.
variants.translation: Yes, the levadas cross the whole island.

dialogues.title: Asking about Madeira's nature
dialogues.context: A tourist asks a local at an information point about the island's natural beauty.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Olá! Queria saber mais sobre a natureza da Madeira.
lines.translation: Hello! I wanted to know more about Madeira's nature.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Pois, a natureza aqui é incrível. Temos a Laurissilva, por exemplo.
lines.translation: Well, the nature here is incredible. We have the Laurel Forest, for example.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Ah, sim! A Laurissilva. É uma floresta antiga, certo?
lines.translation: Ah, yes! The Laurel Forest. It's an ancient forest, right?

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Exato! É Património Mundial. E as levadas? Já conhece?
lines.translation: Exactly! It's a World Heritage Site. And the levadas? Do you know them already?

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Sim, sei que as levadas atravessam a ilha. São boas para caminhar?
lines.translation: Yes, I know the levadas cross the island. Are they good for walking?

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: São ótimas! E a paisagem é de cortar a respiração.
lines.translation: They are great! And the landscape is breathtaking.

roleplay.scenario: You are asking a local for a recommendation for a viewpoint (miradouro) to see the island's beauty.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Can I help you?

options.text: Bom dia! Queria ver um miradouro com uma vista bonita.
options.translation: Good morning! I wanted to see a viewpoint with a beautiful view.
options.feedback: Great start, you asked directly for a viewpoint with a nice view.

options.text: Olá. Onde é a Laurissilva?
options.translation: Hello. Where is the Laurel Forest?
options.feedback: You asked about the Laurel Forest, which is good, but the initial goal was a viewpoint. Let's try to get back on track.

nodes.npc_text: A Laurissilva fica mais para o interior da ilha. Mas se quer uma vista, posso sugerir outra coisa.
nodes.npc_translation: The Laurel Forest is more in the interior of the island. But if you want a view, I can suggest something else.

options.text: Ah, sim. Um miradouro, por favor.
options.translation: Ah, yes. A viewpoint, please.
options.feedback: Good recovery, you redirected to the original goal.

nodes.npc_text: Claro! O Miradouro do Cabo Girão tem uma paisagem de cortar a respiração. Já ouviu falar?
nodes.npc_translation: Of course! The Cabo Girão Viewpoint has a breathtaking landscape. Have you heard of it?

options.text: Não, não conheço. É longe daqui?
options.translation: No, I don't know it. Is it far from here?
options.feedback: Good follow-up question about the location.

options.text: Sim, parece ótimo! Obrigado.
options.translation: Yes, it sounds great! Thank you.
options.feedback: You accepted the suggestion and thanked them. Good, but you could ask for more details.

nodes.npc_text: É um bocado longe, precisa de autocarro ou táxi. Mas vale a pena, a vista é única!
nodes.npc_translation: It's a bit far, you need a bus or taxi. But it's worth it, the view is unique!

options.text: Percebo. Muito obrigado pela ajuda!
options.translation: I understand. Thank you very much for your help!
options.feedback: Excellent, you understood and thanked them politely.

nodes.npc_text: De nada! Tenha um bom dia na nossa ilha.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome! Have a good day on our island.

mission.title: Ask for a recommendation for a scenic walk
prep: Practice asking: 'Queria fazer uma caminhada bonita.' (I wanted to do a beautiful walk.)
Practice asking about levadas: 'As levadas são boas para caminhar, certo?' (The levadas are good for walking, right?)
fallback_phrases: Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?
likely_responses: Há muitas levadas boas, por exemplo a do Caldeirão Verde.

cultural_notes.title: Using 'Pois' for agreement
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, 'Pois' or 'Pois é' is commonly used to express agreement, affirmation, or understanding, similar to 'Indeed,' 'That's right,' or 'Well.' It's a very natural and frequent interjection in daily conversation, especially in Madeira.

cultural_notes.title: The Importance of Levadas
cultural_notes.body: Levadas are unique irrigation channels found on the island of Madeira. They were built to distribute water from the wet north and northwest of the island to the drier south. Today, many of these levadas also serve as walking paths, offering stunning views and a chance to experience the island's unique flora, including the Laurissilva forest.

phrase_patterns.base: É um café que fica perto.

variants.text: É uma loja que tem bons preços.
variants.translation: It's a shop that has good prices.

variants.text: É um restaurante onde se come bem.
variants.translation: It's a restaurant where one eats well.

variants.text: Desde que cheguei, adoro o clima.
variants.translation: Since I arrived, I love the weather.

variants.text: Desde que me mudei, estou mais calmo.
Como não tinha a certeza, liguei para lá.
variants.translation: As I wasn't sure, I called them.

variants.text: Como não percebi, pedi para repetir.
variants.translation: As I didn't understand, I asked them to repeat.

dialogues.title: Booking a cleaning service
dialogues.context: A person is calling a cleaning service to book an appointment.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia! Gostaria de marcar uma limpeza para a minha casa, que fica perto do Funchal.
lines.translation: Good morning! I'd like to book a cleaning for my house, which is near Funchal.

lines.speaker: Serviço
lines.text: Bom dia! Pois, claro. Para quando seria?
lines.translation: Good morning! Yes, of course. For when would it be?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Gostaria que fosse para a próxima semana, se possível. Como não sabia a vossa disponibilidade, liguei a perguntar.
lines.translation: I'd like it to be next week, if possible. As I didn't know your availability, I called to ask.

lines.speaker: Serviço
lines.text: Pois é. Temos vagas na quarta-feira. É um dia que lhe dá jeito?
lines.translation: Indeed. We have openings on Wednesday. Is that a day that suits you?

dialogues.title: Asking about a new shop
dialogues.context: Two neighbours are talking about a new shop that opened.

lines.speaker: Vizinho A
lines.text: Olhe, sabe onde é a nova loja que abriu? Aquela que vende bolos do caco.
lines.translation: Look, do you know where the new shop that opened is? The one that sells bolo do caco.

lines.speaker: Vizinho B
lines.text: Ah, sim! É uma loja que fica na rua principal, perto do mercado. Desde que abriu, está sempre cheia.
lines.translation: Ah, yes! It's a shop that's on the main street, near the market. Since it opened, it's always full.

lines.speaker: Vizinho A
lines.text: Pois, como não sabia onde era, nunca fui lá.
lines.translation: Right, as I didn't know where it was, I never went there.

lines.speaker: Vizinho B
lines.text: Tem de ir! É um sítio onde se come muito bem. E é barato.
lines.translation: You have to go! It's a place where one eats very well. And it's cheap.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a plumber because your kitchen sink is blocked. You need to explain the problem and ask for an appointment.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, canalizador Silva. Diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, plumber Silva. Speak!

options.text: Bom dia. Tenho um problema na cozinha que preciso de resolver.
options.translation: Good morning. I have a problem in the kitchen that I need to solve.
options.feedback: Good start, clearly stating you have a problem.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, diga lá qual é o problema.
nodes.npc_translation: Right, tell me what the problem is.

options.text: É o lava-loiça que está entupido. Desde ontem que não corre a água.
options.translation: It's the sink that's blocked. Since yesterday, the water hasn't been draining.
options.feedback: Excellent, you've explained the specific issue and when it started using 'desde que'.

options.text: A água não está a ir embora no lava-loiça.
options.translation: The water isn't going down in the sink.
options.feedback: This is okay, but try to add more detail next time, like when it started.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, está entupido, então. E desde quando?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, it's blocked then. And since when?

options.text: Desde que cheguei a casa ontem. Como não sabia o que fazer, liguei para si.
options.translation: Since I got home yesterday. As I didn't know what to do, I called you.
options.feedback: Good recovery, you used 'desde que' and 'como não sabia' well.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Para quando gostaria de marcar a visita? Tenho uma vaga amanhã de manhã.
nodes.npc_translation: Right. For when would you like to schedule the visit? I have an opening tomorrow morning.

options.text: Amanhã de manhã é perfeito. Onde é que fica a sua loja, já agora?
options.translation: Tomorrow morning is perfect. Where is your shop, by the way?
options.feedback: You've confirmed the appointment and asked a relevant follow-up question using 'onde'.

options.text: Sim, amanhã de manhã está bem. Obrigado.
options.translation: Yes, tomorrow morning is fine. Thank you.
options.feedback: A polite and clear response. Good job.

nodes.npc_text: A minha loja fica na Rua dos Ferreiros, mas vou diretamente à sua casa. Até amanhã!
nodes.npc_translation: My shop is on Rua dos Ferreiros, but I'll go directly to your house. See you tomorrow!

mission.title: Report a household issue by phone
prep: Practice describing a common household problem (e.g., a broken appliance, a leak, no hot water) using a relative clause: 'Tenho um problema com o frigorífico, que não está a arrefecer.'
Rehearse explaining when the problem started using 'desde que': 'Desde que cheguei a casa, não tenho água quente.'
Prepare to ask for a visit or appointment using 'gostaria de marcar uma visita'.

cultural_notes.title: Using 'Pois' in Conversation
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'Pois é' are very common interjections in Portuguese, especially in Madeira. They can mean 'indeed', 'that's right', 'I see', or simply acknowledge what the other person has said, showing you are listening and understanding. It's often used to agree or confirm information, as seen in the dialogues.

cultural_notes.title: Directness in Requests
cultural_notes.body: While Portuguese can be direct, using 'Gostaria de...' (I would like to...) or 'Podia...?' (Could you...?) is a polite way to make requests, especially when speaking to service providers or people you don't know well. Avoid overly blunt commands, particularly in formal or service interactions.

situations.title: Giving Explanations
situations.summary: Explain things, follow explanations, and check understanding.

variants.translation: I would like to explain to you (formal).

variants.text: Ou seja, o que quer dizer?
variants.translation: In other words, what do you mean?
variants.note: Used to ask for clarification.

variants.translation: In other words, it's like this:

variants.text: Ou seja, é isto que acontece?
variants.translation: In other words, is this what's happening?
variants.note: Used to check understanding.

dialogues.title: Explaining a new bill
dialogues.context: A neighbour is explaining to another neighbour why the electricity bill is higher this month.

lines.speaker: Vizinho A
lines.text: Bom dia, vizinha! Como está? A conta da luz veio com um valor imenso este mês, não percebo.
lines.translation: Good morning, neighbour! How are you? The electricity bill came with a huge amount this month, I don't understand.

lines.speaker: Vizinho B
lines.text: Pois é, a minha também. Deixa-me explicar o que me disseram na loja. Basicamente, aumentaram os preços.
lines.translation: Indeed, mine too. Let me explain what they told me at the shop. Basically, they increased the prices.

lines.speaker: Vizinho A
lines.text: Aumentaram? Ou seja, vamos pagar mais por tudo?
lines.translation: Increased? In other words, we're going to pay more for everything?

lines.speaker: Vizinho B
lines.text: Exato. Por exemplo, a tarifa noturna está mais cara. Faz sentido para si?
lines.translation: Exactly. For example, the night tariff is more expensive. Does that make sense to you?

lines.speaker: Vizinho A
lines.text: Faz, faz. É um bocado chato, mas pronto. Obrigado por explicar!
lines.translation: Yes, it does. It's a bit annoying, but oh well. Thanks for explaining!

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a repair service about a broken washing machine. You need to explain the problem and understand the next steps.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde, Serviço de Reparações. Diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon, Repair Service. Speak!

options.text: A minha máquina de lavar está com um problema.
options.translation: My washing machine has a problem.
options.feedback: Good, you've stated the problem directly.

options.text: Gostaria de explicar uma situação.
options.translation: I would like to explain a situation.
options.feedback: A bit formal, but acceptable. The next step is to explain the problem.

nodes.npc_text: Um problema? Deixa-me explicar, preciso de saber qual é o modelo e o que está a acontecer.
nodes.npc_translation: A problem? Let me explain, I need to know the model and what is happening.

options.text: É uma máquina antiga, e não está a deitar a água fora, por exemplo.
options.translation: It's an old machine, and it's not draining the water, for example.
options.feedback: Excellent, you've given a specific detail and used 'por exemplo'.

options.text: Ou seja, a água não sai, está lá dentro.
options.translation: In other words, the water isn't coming out, it's inside.
options.feedback: Good use of 'ou seja' to clarify the problem.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, percebo. Basicamente, pode ser a bomba. Faz sentido para si? Não consigo dar um diagnóstico sem ver.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, I understand. Basically, it could be the pump. Does that make sense to you? I can't give a diagnosis without seeing it.

options.text: Sim, faz sentido. Quando pode vir?
options.translation: Yes, it makes sense. When can you come?
options.feedback: Good, you've confirmed understanding and asked about the next step.

options.text: Ou seja, tem de vir cá para ver o que é?
options.translation: In other words, you have to come here to see what it is?
options.feedback: Correct, you've used 'ou seja' to confirm the process.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é. Posso ir amanhã de manhã, entre as nove e o meio-dia.
nodes.npc_translation: Indeed. I can come tomorrow morning, between nine and noon.

options.text: Perfeito! Então, amanhã de manhã.
options.translation: Perfect! So, tomorrow morning.
options.feedback: Great, you've confirmed the appointment.

options.text: Obrigado!
options.translation: Thank you!
options.feedback: A polite way to end the call.

mission.title: Explain a household issue to a neighbour
prep: Think of a small household problem (e.g., a noisy tap, a broken light bulb, a recycling question).
Imagine you need to explain it to a neighbour who might not know the details.
Practice using 'Deixa-me explicar.', 'Ou seja...', and 'Faz sentido?' in your explanation.

cultural_notes.title: Checking Understanding in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, it's very common and polite to explicitly check if someone understands your explanation by asking 'Faz sentido?' (Does it make sense?) or 'Percebes?' (Do you understand? - informal). This shows you care about clear communication and gives the other person an opportunity to ask questions.

cultural_notes.title: The Versatile 'Pois é'
cultural_notes.body: The phrase 'Pois é' is frequently used in Madeiran and European Portuguese. It can mean 'Indeed', 'That's right', 'Exactly', or simply acknowledge what the other person has said without necessarily agreeing. It's a natural filler that adds to the flow of conversation and often softens the tone.

dialogues.title: High Water Bill
dialogues.context: A customer calls Águas da Madeira (the water company) about an unusually high bill.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Águas da Madeira, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Madeira Water, good afternoon. How can I help you?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa tarde. O meu nome é [nome] e liguei por causa de uma fatura.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. My name is [name] and I called because of a bill.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Pois não. Qual é o número da fatura, por favor?
lines.translation: Yes, of course. What is the bill number, please?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: É a fatura número 2023/12345. Está muito alta este mês.
lines.translation: It's bill number 2023/12345. It's very high this month.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Um momento, por favor... Ah, sim. Vejo aqui. Houve um consumo um bocado elevado.
lines.translation: One moment, please... Ah, yes. I see here. There was a somewhat high consumption.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Pois é, mas não me parece normal. Não estivemos cá muito tempo.
lines.translation: Indeed, but it doesn't seem normal to me. We weren't here much time.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Entendo. Quer que enviemos um técnico para verificar o contador?
lines.translation: I understand. Do you want us to send a technician to check the meter?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, por favor. Pode ser na próxima semana?
lines.translation: Yes, please. Can it be next week?

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Podemos agendar para terça-feira de manhã, entre as nove e o meio-dia.
lines.translation: We can schedule it for Tuesday morning, between nine and noon.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Terça-feira de manhã, ótimo. Obrigada.
lines.translation: Tuesday morning, great. Thank you.

dialogues.title: Confirming Cleaning
dialogues.context: A person is confirming a cleaning appointment with their cleaner, D. Maria.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Olá, D. Maria. Tudo bem?
lines.translation: Hello, D. Maria. How are you?

lines.speaker: Empregada
lines.text: Olá! Tudo bem, obrigada. É para confirmar a limpeza para amanhã, pois é?
lines.translation: Hello! I'm fine, thank you. It's to confirm the cleaning for tomorrow, right?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Sim, isso mesmo. Amanhã de manhã, às nove, está bem?
lines.translation: Yes, that's right. Tomorrow morning, at nine, is that okay?

lines.speaker: Empregada
lines.text: Sim, sim, às nove está perfeito. Não há problema nenhum.
lines.translation: Yes, yes, nine o'clock is perfect. No problem at all.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Ótimo. Precisa de alguma coisa em especial?
lines.translation: Great. Do you need anything special?

lines.speaker: Empregada
lines.text: Não, tá tudo bem. Tenho os meus produtos. Só preciso que me diga se há alguma coisa que queira que eu faça diferente.
lines.translation: No, everything's fine. I have my products. I just need you to tell me if there's anything you want me to do differently.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Não, está tudo como sempre. Obrigada, D. Maria.
lines.translation: No, everything is as always. Thank you, D. Maria.

lines.speaker: Empregada
lines.text: De nada! Até amanhã.
lines.translation: You're welcome! See you tomorrow.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling an appliance repair service because your washing machine is broken. You need to explain the problem and schedule a repair.

nodes.npc_text: Assistência Técnica, boa tarde. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Technical Assistance, good afternoon. How can I help you?

options.text: Boa tarde. A minha máquina de lavar roupa está avariada.
options.translation: Good afternoon. My washing machine is broken.
options.feedback: You've clearly stated the problem. Good start!

options.text: Olá. Preciso de um técnico para a máquina de lavar.
options.translation: Hello. I need a technician for the washing machine.
options.feedback: Direct and clear. Now you'll need to explain the issue.

nodes.npc_text: Pois não. Qual é o problema, exatamente?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, of course. What is the problem, exactly?

options.text: Ela não liga de todo.
options.translation: It doesn't turn on at all.
options.feedback: Good, you've specified the fault.

options.text: Ela faz muito barulho e não centrifuga.
options.translation: It makes a lot of noise and doesn't spin.
options.feedback: Clear explanation of the symptoms.

options.text: Está a deitar água para fora.
options.translation: It's leaking water.
options.feedback: A common and easy-to-understand problem description.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Podemos agendar uma visita para amanhã de manhã, entre as nove e o meio-dia, ou quinta-feira à tarde?
nodes.npc_translation: Right. We can schedule a visit for tomorrow morning, between nine and noon, or Thursday afternoon?

options.text: Amanhã de manhã está ótimo.
options.translation: Tomorrow morning is great.
options.feedback: Excellent, you've accepted the first option.

options.text: Quinta-feira à tarde é melhor.
options.translation: Thursday afternoon is better.
options.feedback: Good, you've chosen the second option.

options.text: Não consigo amanhã nem quinta. Tem outra disponibilidade?
options.translation: I can't tomorrow or Thursday. Do you have other availability?
options.feedback: Well done for asking for more options! This shows flexibility.

nodes.npc_text: Perfeito. Então fica agendado para amanhã de manhã. O técnico liga antes de ir.
nodes.npc_translation: Perfect. So it's scheduled for tomorrow morning. The technician will call before going.

nodes.npc_text: Perfeito. Então fica agendado para quinta-feira à tarde. O técnico liga antes de ir.
nodes.npc_translation: Perfect. So it's scheduled for Thursday afternoon. The technician will call before going.

nodes.npc_text: Um momento, por favor... Temos na sexta-feira de manhã, entre as dez e a uma.
nodes.npc_translation: One moment, please... We have Friday morning, between ten and one.

options.text: Sexta-feira de manhã está bem. Obrigada.
options.translation: Friday morning is fine. Thank you.
options.feedback: Great, you've found a suitable time and confirmed.

options.text: Não, também não dá. Vou ligar mais tarde.
options.translation: No, that doesn't work either. I'll call later.
options.feedback: It's okay to end the call if no time works. You handled it politely.

nodes.npc_text: Ótimo. Fica agendado para sexta-feira de manhã. O técnico liga antes de ir.
nodes.npc_translation: Great. It's scheduled for Friday morning. The technician will call before going.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Ligue quando tiver outra disponibilidade. Boa tarde.
nodes.npc_translation: Right. Call when you have other availability. Good afternoon.

mission.title: Schedule a Washing Machine Repair
prep: Think about how to explain the problem with your washing machine simply.
Practice asking for alternative times if the first ones don't work for you.
Remember to confirm the appointment time and day clearly.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Staff
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, especially in Madeira, it's common and polite to address service staff (e.g., shopkeepers, receptionists, technicians) using 'o senhor' (for men) or 'a senhora' (for women). If you know their first name and they are older, using 'Dona' (for women) or 'Dom' (for men) followed by their first name is also a sign of respect. Avoid using 'você' in these contexts as it can sometimes sound too direct or even impolite, especially with strangers or in formal settings.

cultural_notes.title: The Versatile 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: The word 'pois' is very common in Portuguese conversations, particularly in Madeira, and its meaning varies with context. 'Pois não' is frequently used by service staff to mean 'Yes, how can I help you?' or 'Of course, what can I do for you?', signaling readiness to assist. 'Pois é' is used to express agreement, acknowledgement, or to confirm something, similar to 'That's right' or 'Indeed' in English. Pay attention to the intonation for subtle differences in meaning.

dialogues.title: Welcoming the Cleaners
dialogues.context: Two cleaners arrive at a house.

lines.speaker: Dono da casa
lines.text: Bom dia! Entrem, por favor. Vocês são a equipa de limpeza, certo?
lines.translation: Good morning! Come in, please. You are the cleaning team, right?

lines.speaker: Chefe da equipa
lines.text: Pois é, somos nós. Bom dia. Onde é que vocês querem que a gente comece?
lines.translation: That's right, it's us. Good morning. Where do you all want us to start?

lines.speaker: Dono da casa
lines.text: Primeiro, a cozinha e as casas de banho. Depois, os quartos. Vocês têm tudo o que precisam?
lines.translation: First, the kitchen and the bathrooms. Then, the bedrooms. Do you all have everything you need?

lines.speaker: Chefe da equipa
lines.text: Sim, sim, temos. Não se preocupem. Vocês querem que a gente leve o lixo?
lines.translation: Yes, yes, we have. Don't worry. Do you all want us to take the rubbish?

lines.speaker: Dono da casa
lines.text: Sim, por favor. E depois, vocês querem café ou água?
lines.translation: Yes, please. And afterwards, do you all want coffee or water?

dialogues.title: Furniture Delivery
dialogues.context: Two delivery workers are bringing furniture into an apartment.

lines.speaker: Dono da casa
lines.text: Olá! Sejam bem-vindos! Vocês estão a precisar de ajuda com isso?
lines.translation: Hello! Welcome! Do you all need help with that?

lines.speaker: Entregador 1
lines.text: Boa tarde. Não, obrigado, nós temos. Onde é que vocês querem que a gente ponha este sofá?
lines.translation: Good afternoon. No, thanks, we're fine. Where do you all want us to put this sofa?

lines.speaker: Dono da casa
lines.text: O sofá é na sala, ali. E as cadeiras, vocês podem pôr na cozinha.
lines.translation: The sofa is in the living room, over there. And the chairs, you all can put them in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Entregador 2
lines.text: Perfeito. Vocês têm mais alguma coisa para nós?
lines.translation: Perfect. Do you all have anything else for us?

lines.speaker: Dono da casa
lines.text: Não, é só isso. Muito obrigado! Vocês foram muito rápidos!
lines.translation: No, that's all. Thank you very much! You all were very fast!

roleplay.scenario: You are at home and a team of two plumbers arrives to fix a leak. You need to direct them and offer them something to drink.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde! Nós somos os canalizadores. Vocês ligaram por causa da fuga de água, certo?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon! We are the plumbers. You all called about the water leak, right?

options.text: Sim, sou eu. Entrem, por favor.
options.translation: Yes, it's me. Come in, please.
options.feedback: Good, you confirmed and invited them in.

options.text: Quem são vocês?
options.translation: Who are you all?
options.feedback: A bit direct. Try to be more welcoming.

nodes.npc_text: Como disse, somos os canalizadores. Vieram para a fuga. Podemos entrar?
nodes.npc_translation: As I said, we are the plumbers. We came for the leak. May we come in?

options.text: Ah, sim, desculpem. Entrem, por favor.
options.translation: Oh, yes, sorry. Come in, please.
options.feedback: Better, you apologized and invited them in.

nodes.npc_text: Onde é que vocês querem que a gente veja a fuga?
nodes.npc_translation: Where do you all want us to check for the leak?

options.text: É na casa de banho, no andar de cima.
options.translation: It's in the bathroom, upstairs.
options.feedback: Clear directions.

options.text: Ali.
options.translation: There.
options.feedback: Try to be more specific with words, not just pointing.

nodes.npc_text: Onde 'ali'? No andar de baixo ou no andar de cima?
nodes.npc_translation: Where 'there'? Downstairs or upstairs?

options.text: Ah, desculpem. É na casa de banho, no andar de cima.
options.translation: Oh, sorry. It's in the bathroom, upstairs.
options.feedback: Good, you clarified.

nodes.npc_text: Ok, vamos lá ver. Vocês têm um balde ou toalhas velhas?
nodes.npc_translation: Okay, let's go check. Do you all have a bucket or old towels?

options.text: Sim, eu trago já. E depois, vocês querem café ou água?
options.translation: Yes, I'll bring them now. And afterwards, do you all want coffee or water?
options.feedback: Excellent, you offered refreshments using 'vocês'.

options.text: Sim, tenho. Já trago.
options.translation: Yes, I have. I'll bring them now.
options.feedback: Good, but you missed an opportunity to offer a drink.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigado. Está bem, a gente avisa quando acabar. Vocês precisam de alguma coisa?
nodes.npc_translation: Thanks. Okay, we'll let you know when we finish. Do you all need anything?

options.text: Não, obrigado. Mas, vocês querem café ou água?
options.translation: No, thanks. But, do you all want coffee or water?
options.feedback: Good, you remembered to offer a drink.

options.text: Não, obrigado.
options.translation: No, thanks.
options.feedback: You missed an opportunity to offer a drink, which is common hospitality.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, muito obrigado! Um café, por favor. Vocês são muito simpáticos!
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, thank you very much! A coffee, please. You all are very kind!

mission.title: Directing a Maintenance Crew
prep: Practice inviting people in: 'Entrem, por favor!'
Rehearse asking if they need anything: 'Vocês precisam de alguma coisa?'
Prepare to offer drinks: 'Vocês querem café ou água?'
likely_responses: Onde é que é o problema?

cultural_notes.title: Offering Refreshments
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, and Portugal generally, it's very common and polite to offer visitors, especially service people like plumbers or delivery staff, a coffee, water, or juice. It's a small gesture of hospitality that is much appreciated.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Groups with 'Vocês'
cultural_notes.body: While 'vocês' is grammatically the plural 'you,' its social register can vary. When addressing a group of service providers, it's neutral and appropriate. If you were addressing a group of close friends, it would be informal. Be mindful of context, but for service interactions, it's perfectly fine.

variants.translation: When you go to Funchal, tell me.

dialogues.title: Scheduling a cleaner
dialogues.context: A homeowner calling a cleaning service to arrange a future visit.

lines.speaker: Serviço
lines.text: Boa tarde, Serviço de Limpezas. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Good afternoon, Cleaning Service. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Boa tarde. Gostaria de agendar uma limpeza. Quando a senhora tiver disponibilidade, pode vir na próxima semana?
lines.translation: Good afternoon. I'd like to schedule a cleaning. When you (formal) have availability, can you come next week?

lines.speaker: Serviço
lines.text: Claro. Assim que tivermos a agenda da semana, avisamos. Quando o senhor for à Madeira, podemos combinar.
lines.translation: Of course. As soon as we have the week's schedule, we'll let you know. When you (formal) come to Madeira, we can arrange it.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Pois, obrigada. Assim que souber, diga, por favor.
lines.translation: Right, thank you. As soon as you know, please tell me.

dialogues.title: Friends planning a visit
dialogues.context: Two friends discussing plans for when one visits the other in Madeira.

lines.speaker: Amigo 1
lines.text: Então, quando vieres à Madeira, o que queres fazer?
lines.translation: So, when you come to Madeira, what do you want to do?

lines.speaker: Amigo 2
lines.text: Ah, não sei! Assim que chegar, quero ir à praia. E se pudermos, uma levada!
lines.translation: Oh, I don't know! As soon as I arrive, I want to go to the beach. And if we can, a levada!

lines.speaker: Amigo 1
lines.text: Pois, claro! Quando fores à praia, diz-me. Eu vou contigo. E quando tiveres tempo, vamos à levada.
lines.translation: Right, of course! When you go to the beach, tell me. I'll go with you. And when you have time, we'll go to the levada.

lines.speaker: Amigo 2
lines.text: Combinado! Assim que o meu voo for confirmado, aviso-te.
lines.translation: Deal! As soon as my flight is confirmed, I'll let you know.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a plumber to fix a leaky tap in your apartment. You need to arrange a time for them to come.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde, Canalizações Rápidas. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon, Quick Plumbing. How can I help?

options.text: Boa tarde. Tenho uma torneira a pingar. Gostaria de agendar uma visita.
options.translation: Good afternoon. I have a dripping tap. I'd like to schedule a visit.
options.feedback: Good start, clear and polite.

options.text: Olá. A minha torneira tá estragada. Podem vir?
options.translation: Hi. My tap is broken. Can you come?
options.feedback: A bit informal, but understandable. Try to be a bit more polite for a service call.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, claro. Qual é a sua morada? E quando o senhor tiver um tempinho, qual seria a melhor hora para a visita?
nodes.npc_translation: Right, of course. What's your address? And when you (formal) have a little time, what would be the best time for the visit?

options.text: A morada é Rua da Calçada, número 10. Se puderem vir amanhã de manhã, era ótimo.
options.translation: The address is Rua da Calçada, number 10. If you can come tomorrow morning, that would be great.
options.feedback: Excellent, clear and uses 'se puderem'.

options.text: Moro na Rua da Calçada, 10. Amanhã de manhã, se der.
options.translation: I live on Rua da Calçada, 10. Tomorrow morning, if it works.
options.feedback: A bit abrupt, but they understood. 'Se der' is a common informal reduction of 'se der jeito' (if it's convenient).

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã de manhã, então. Assim que o canalizador estiver a caminho, ligamos-lhe para avisar.
nodes.npc_translation: Tomorrow morning, then. As soon as the plumber is on the way, we'll call you to let you know.

options.text: Muito obrigada! Fico à espera.
options.translation: Thank you very much! I'll be waiting.
options.feedback: Perfect ending, polite and natural.

options.text: Ok, obrigada.
options.translation: Okay, thanks.
options.feedback: Simple and effective ending.

mission.title: Arrange a delivery
prep: Imagine you've ordered some furniture. You need to call the shop to arrange delivery.
Think about what day and time you prefer for delivery.
Practice asking 'Quando puderem entregar?' (When can you deliver?) or 'Se tiverem disponibilidade...' (If you have availability...).
likely_responses: Quando é que o senhor está em casa?

cultural_notes.title: Politeness with 'o senhor' / 'a senhora'
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, especially in service interactions or with people you don't know well, it's common and polite to use 'o senhor' (you, masculine formal) or 'a senhora' (you, feminine formal) instead of 'tu' (you, informal). This shows respect and avoids being overly familiar. It's often paired with the third person singular verb form.

cultural_notes.title: Using 'Pois' / 'Pois é'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'Pois é' are very common interjections in Portuguese. They don't always translate directly but often mean 'Right', 'Indeed', 'Exactly', or simply acknowledge what the other person said, showing you're listening and agreeing or understanding. It's a natural part of conversation.

dialogues.title: Arranging a Cleaner
dialogues.context: A homeowner calls a cleaning service to arrange a visit.

lines.speaker: Homeowner
lines.text: Boa tarde. Para a senhora vir limpar, quando é que é melhor?
lines.translation: Good afternoon. For you to come clean, when is it best?

lines.speaker: Cleaner
lines.text: Boa tarde. Para eu vir, a terça-feira de manhã é bom.
lines.translation: Good afternoon. For me to come, Tuesday morning is good.

lines.speaker: Homeowner
lines.text: Pois, e para nós falarmos sobre o trabalho?
lines.translation: Right, and for us to talk about the work?

lines.speaker: Cleaner
lines.text: Para nós falarmos, podemos combinar quando eu chegar.
lines.translation: For us to talk, we can arrange it when I arrive.

dialogues.title: Confirming a Delivery
dialogues.context: A customer calls a store about a fridge delivery.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Bom dia. Ligo sobre a entrega do frigorífico. Para eles entregarem, qual é o horário?
lines.translation: Good morning. I'm calling about the fridge delivery. For them to deliver, what's the schedule?

lines.speaker: Service Assistant
lines.text: Bom dia. Para eles poderem entregar, é entre as duas e as quatro da tarde.
lines.translation: Good morning. For them to be able to deliver, it's between two and four in the afternoon.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Pois, antes de eu sair de casa, preciso de ter a certeza.
lines.translation: Right, before I leave home, I need to be sure.

lines.speaker: Service Assistant
lines.text: P'ra eles virem, tem de estar lá alguém, pois.
lines.translation: For them to come, someone has to be there, right.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a plumber to fix a leak in your house. You need to arrange a time for him to visit.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde. Diga! Para eu ir ver a fuga, quando é que é melhor para si?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon. Tell me! For me to go see the leak, when is it best for you?

options.text: Para o senhor vir, a tarde de amanhã é boa.
options.translation: For you to come, tomorrow afternoon is good.
options.feedback: Good, you've suggested a time using the personal infinitive.

options.text: Quando é que é bom para o senhor?
options.translation: When is it good for you?
options.feedback: Asking about their availability is also a good option.

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã à tarde, pois é bom. Antes de eu ir, pode confirmar a morada?
nodes.npc_translation: Tomorrow afternoon, that's good. Before I go, can you confirm the address?

options.text: Sim, para confirmarmos tudo, a morada é Rua da Paz, número 5.
options.translation: Yes, for us to confirm everything, the address is Rua da Paz, number 5.
options.feedback: Excellent, you confirmed the address and used the personal infinitive.

options.text: Para eu confirmar, preciso de um minuto.
options.translation: For me to confirm, I need a minute.
options.feedback: It's good to be clear if you need a moment. You used the personal infinitive correctly.

nodes.npc_text: Para eu poder ir, tenho a manhã de quinta-feira livre.
nodes.npc_translation: For me to be able to go, I have Thursday morning free.

options.text: Quinta-feira de manhã é bom para mim.
options.translation: Thursday morning is good for me.
options.feedback: Great, you agreed to the time. Now confirm the address.

options.text: Para nós falarmos melhor, posso ligar mais tarde?
options.translation: For us to speak better, can I call later?
options.feedback: It's fine to defer the call if you need more time to check your schedule.

nodes.npc_text: P'ra já, muito obrigado. Até amanhã à tarde então.
nodes.npc_translation: For now, thank you very much. See you tomorrow afternoon then.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. Para eu não ir para a morada errada, é mesmo importante.
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. For me not to go to the wrong address, it's really important.

options.text: A morada é Rua da Paz, número 5.
options.translation: The address is Rua da Paz, number 5.
options.feedback: Perfect, you've given the address. The appointment is set!

nodes.npc_text: Está bem, ligue quando puder. Bom dia.
nodes.npc_translation: Alright, call when you can. Good day.

mission.title: Arrange a Home Repair
prep: Imagine you have a problem at home (e.g., a leaking tap, a broken washing machine).
Call a local repair service (e.g., canalizador for plumber, eletricista for electrician).
Clearly explain the problem.
Use phrases with the personal infinitive to discuss when it's best for the professional to come, and for you to be at home.
Confirm the appointment details (date, time, address).
fallback_phrases: Não percebi, pode falar mais devagar?
Quando é que é melhor para o senhor/a senhora vir?
likely_responses: Para eu ir ver o problema...
Para o senhor/a senhora estar em casa...

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Service Requests
cultural_notes.body: When arranging services in Portugal, using the personal infinitive construction like 'Para o senhor vir...' (For you to come...) is a polite and common way to make a request or inquire about availability. It frames the action as a condition or purpose, which softens the request compared to a more direct 'Quando pode vir?' (When can you come?). This indirectness is often preferred in formal or service interactions.

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Service Professionals
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, it is customary and polite to address service professionals (plumbers, cleaners, delivery drivers, shop assistants) using 'o senhor' (the sir) or 'a senhora' (the madam) with the third-person verb form. This shows respect for their professional role and is generally preferred over using 'tu' (you, informal) or 'você' (you, which can sound distant in Portugal).

Say this phrase from memory: "Para podermos ir..."
Say this phrase from memory: "É importante falarmos."

situations.title: Abstract Topics: Work & Decisions
situations.summary: Discuss professional topics, decisions, and plans.

variants.text: Comecei a trabalhar por conta própria.
Estou a pensar trabalhar por conta própria.
variants.translation: I'm thinking of working for myself.
I need to decide to change my life.

variants.text: Estou a pensar mudar de emprego.
Quero mudar de vida, mas é difícil.
variants.translation: I want to change my life, but it's difficult.
I think I don't regret it.

dialogues.title: Career Changes and Burnout
dialogues.context: Two friends, Ana and Carlos, are having coffee and discussing Carlos's new work situation.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Então, Carlos, como é que vai o teu negócio novo?
lines.translation: So, Carlos, how is your new business going?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Pois, Ana, tá a correr bem, mas dá trabalho. Decidi mudar de vida, não é?
lines.translation: Well, Ana, it's going well, but it's a lot of work. I decided to change my life, didn't I?

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: E não te arrependes de ter deixado o teletrabalho?
lines.translation: And you don't regret leaving remote work?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Não me arrependo nada. O esgotamento era demais. Agora trabalho por conta própria e tenho mais liberdade.
lines.translation: I don't regret it at all. The burnout was too much. Now I work for myself and have more freedom.

dialogues.title: The Decision to Work Independently
dialogues.context: Sofia and Miguel, colleagues, are talking during lunch about Miguel's decision to work for himself.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Diz-me, Miguel, como é que decidiste trabalhar por conta própria?
lines.translation: Tell me, Miguel, how did you decide to work for yourself?

lines.speaker: Miguel
lines.text: Foi depois de um período de esgotamento. Precisava de algo diferente.
lines.translation: It was after a period of burnout. I needed something different.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: E não te arrependes dessa decisão?
lines.translation: And you don't regret that decision?

lines.speaker: Miguel
lines.text: Não, de todo. É mais difícil às vezes, mas tenho mais controlo. Foi a melhor decisão.
lines.translation: No, not at all. It's harder sometimes, but I have more control. It was the best decision.

roleplay.scenario: You are talking to your friend Joana about your work. She knows you recently made a big change and is asking about it.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Então, como é que está a correr o teu novo trabalho? Ou o teu negócio?
nodes.npc_translation: Hi! So, how's your new job going? Or your business?

options.text: Decidi mudar de vida e agora trabalho por conta própria.
options.translation: I decided to change my life and now I work for myself.
options.feedback: Great, you clearly stated your decision and new work status.

options.text: Está a correr bem, obrigado. É um desafio.
options.translation: It's going well, thanks. It's a challenge.
options.feedback: You answered politely, but didn't give much detail. Joana will ask more.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, que bom! E porquê essa mudança? Não estavas feliz no teletrabalho?
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, that's good! And why the change? Weren't you happy with remote work?

options.text: Não, o teletrabalho causava-me muito esgotamento.
options.translation: No, remote work caused me a lot of burnout.
options.feedback: You explained the reason clearly, using the vocabulary 'esgotamento'.

options.text: Queria mais liberdade e um negócio meu.
options.translation: I wanted more freedom and a business of my own.
options.feedback: Good, you stated your motivation for the change.

nodes.npc_text: Um desafio, pois é. Mas o que te fez decidir mudar de vida e deixar o que fazias antes?
nodes.npc_translation: A challenge, indeed. But what made you decide to change your life and leave what you were doing before?

options.text: Estava com esgotamento e precisava de uma mudança.
options.translation: I was experiencing burnout and needed a change.
options.feedback: Direct and clear answer using 'esgotamento'.

options.text: Queria começar o meu próprio negócio.
options.translation: I wanted to start my own business.
options.feedback: Good, you explained your goal.

nodes.npc_text: Percebo. E não te arrependes de ter tomado essa decisão?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. And you don't regret having made that decision?

options.text: Não, não me arrependo nada. Foi a melhor coisa.
options.translation: No, I don't regret it at all. It was the best thing.
options.feedback: Excellent, a strong and positive affirmation.

options.text: Acho que não me arrependo. É bom ter o meu próprio negócio.
options.translation: I think I don't regret it. It's good to have my own business.
options.feedback: A slightly more hesitant but still positive answer. Good.

nodes.npc_text: Que bom! Fico feliz por ti. Boa sorte com tudo!
nodes.npc_translation: That's great! I'm happy for you. Good luck with everything!

nodes.npc_text: Pois, ter o nosso próprio negócio é sempre bom. Desejo-te o melhor.
nodes.npc_translation: Well, having our own business is always good. I wish you the best.

mission.title: Discussing a Recent Career Change
prep: Think about your reasons for changing jobs or starting a business.
Prepare to explain if you regret your decision or not.
Consider how to use phrases like 'trabalho por conta própria' and 'esgotamento'.

cultural_notes.title: Discussing 'Esgotamento' (Burnout)
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, 'esgotamento' (burnout) is a recognized and increasingly discussed issue, particularly in urban and professional contexts. It's common to hear people talk about needing a change or a break due to stress, though some may still be reserved about openly discussing mental health. Using the term is generally understood and can be a valid reason for career changes.

cultural_notes.title: 'Trabalhar por conta própria' vs. 'Ser empregado'
cultural_notes.body: The distinction between 'trabalhar por conta própria' (working for oneself, self-employed) and 'ser empregado' (being employed by someone else) is very common in Portuguese conversations about work. While traditional employment is still prevalent, there's a growing trend, especially among younger generations, towards entrepreneurship and independent work, often driven by a desire for more flexibility or control, as well as sometimes by economic necessity.

situations.title: Formal Writing & Funchal
situations.summary: Draft formal messages and discuss Funchal local geography.

variants.text: Venho por este meio informar que a fatura foi paga.
variants.translation: I am writing to inform you that the invoice has been paid.

variants.text: Venho por este meio solicitar a sua intervenção.
variants.translation: I am writing to request your intervention.

variants.text: Venho por este meio agradecer a sua pronta resposta.
variants.translation: I am writing to thank you for your prompt reply.

variants.text: Agradecia se pudesse enviar o comprovativo.
variants.translation: I would be grateful if you could send the proof.

variants.text: Agradecia se pudesse verificar o meu pedido.
variants.translation: I would be grateful if you could check my request.
I would be grateful if you could contact me tomorrow.

variants.note: A very common formal closing in Portugal.
Slightly less formal but still polite.

dialogues.title: Funchal Landmarks
dialogues.context: A tourist asks a local for directions to famous spots in Funchal.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Bom dia, desculpe. Onde fica a Sé?
lines.translation: Good morning, excuse me. Where is the Cathedral?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: A Sé? Pois, é fácil. É mesmo ali, na baixa. Siga em frente.
lines.translation: The Cathedral? Right, it's easy. It's right there, downtown. Go straight ahead.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: E a zona velha? É perto do teleférico?
lines.translation: And the old town? Is it near the cable car?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Sim, a zona velha é junto ao teleférico, mesmo ao lado. Não tem como enganar.
lines.translation: Yes, the old town is next to the cable car, right beside it. You can't miss it.

dialogues.title: Confirming a Payment by Phone
dialogues.context: A person calls a customer service to confirm a bill payment.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Serviço de Apoio ao Cliente, bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Customer Support Service, good morning. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia. Venho por este meio confirmar o pagamento da fatura número dois, três, quatro, cinco.
lines.translation: Good morning. I am calling to confirm the payment of invoice number two, three, four, five.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Um momento, por favor... Sim, está tudo em ordem. O pagamento foi registado.
lines.translation: One moment, please... Yes, everything is in order. The payment has been registered.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Agradecia se pudesse enviar um comprovativo por email.
lines.translation: I would be grateful if you could send a proof by email.

lines.speaker: Atendente
lines.text: Pois, claro. Qual é o seu endereço de email, por favor?
lines.translation: Yes, of course. What is your email address, please?

roleplay.scenario: You are calling the Funchal Municipal Services to report a broken street light in your neighbourhood. You need to make a formal request and provide details.

nodes.npc_text: Serviços Municipais do Funchal, bom dia. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Funchal Municipal Services, good morning. How can I help?

options.text: Bom dia. Venho por este meio reportar uma avaria na iluminação pública.
options.translation: Good morning. I am calling to report a fault in the public lighting.
options.feedback: Excellent formal opening. Now provide more details about the problem.

options.text: Olá. A luz da rua está estragada.
options.translation: Hi. The street light is broken.
options.feedback: This is too informal for a municipal service. Try to use a more formal opening.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E qual é a morada da avaria, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: Understood. And what is the address of the fault, please?

options.text: A avaria é na Rua da Levada, número vinte e cinco. O poste está mesmo em frente à casa.
options.translation: The fault is on Rua da Levada, number twenty-five. The pole is right in front of the house.
options.feedback: Good, you provided the necessary details clearly.

options.text: É numa rua perto da Sé. Não sei o número.
options.translation: It's on a street near the Cathedral. I don't know the number.
options.feedback: You need to be more specific. Try to find the exact address or a clear landmark.

nodes.npc_text: Pode ser mais preciso, por favor? Precisamos da morada exata para enviar uma equipa.
nodes.npc_translation: Can you be more precise, please? We need the exact address to send a team.

options.text: Desculpe. É na Rua da Levada, número vinte e cinco. O poste está mesmo em frente à casa.
options.translation: Sorry. It's on Rua da Levada, number twenty-five. The pole is right in front of the house.
options.feedback: Much better! Providing a precise address is crucial.

options.text: Não consigo. Vou ligar mais tarde.
options.translation: I can't. I'll call later.
options.feedback: It's okay to end the call if you don't have the information, but try to be polite.

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. Registámos o seu pedido. Uma equipa irá verificar a situação o mais breve possível. Agradecemos o seu contacto.
nodes.npc_translation: Very good. We have registered your request. A team will check the situation as soon as possible. We thank you for your contact.

options.text: Agradecia se pudesse enviar uma notificação quando estiver resolvido.
options.translation: I would be grateful if you could send a notification when it's resolved.
options.feedback: A good, polite follow-up request. Now conclude the call.

options.text: Obrigado.
options.translation: Thank you.
options.feedback: A simple 'thank you' is fine, but you could add a formal closing.

nodes.npc_text: De nada. Tenha um bom dia.
nodes.npc_translation: You're welcome. Have a good day.

options.text: Muito obrigado. Com os melhores cumprimentos, tenha um bom dia.
options.translation: Thank you very much. With best regards, have a good day.
options.feedback: A perfect, polite closing for a formal call.

mission.title: Report a Public Service Issue Formally
prep: Practice using 'Venho por este meio...' to state your purpose clearly.
Prepare to give specific details like an address or location.
Rehearse formal phrases like 'Agradecia se pudesse...' for requests.
Practice polite greetings and closings for a phone call.
fallback_phrases: Não percebi bem. Pode falar mais devagar?
Qual é o seu nome, por favor?
likely_responses: Em que zona da cidade é?

cultural_notes.title: Formalidade no Telefone em Portugal
cultural_notes.body: When calling official services or businesses in Portugal, it's common to maintain a formal tone. Always start with a 'Bom dia' or 'Boa tarde', and use 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' even if you don't know the person. Avoid overly casual language. Formal closings like 'Com os melhores cumprimentos' or 'Atenciosamente' are also appropriate, even on the phone, before saying 'Tenha um bom dia'.

cultural_notes.title: Estrutura de um Pedido Formal
cultural_notes.body: A formal request often follows a clear structure: 1. State the purpose directly using phrases like 'Venho por este meio informar que...' or 'Venho por este meio solicitar...'. 2. Provide all necessary details clearly and concisely. 3. Make any specific requests using 'Agradecia se pudesse...'. 4. Conclude with a polite thank you and a formal closing.
