situations.title: Local Beaches & Hiking
situations.summary: Speak about Madeiran outdoor life with genuine local knowledge.

phrase_patterns.base: Para praia de areia, recomendo Calheta.

variants.text: Para praia de seixo, recomendo a Formosa.
variants.translation: For a pebble beach, I recommend Formosa.

variants.text: Para um bom trilho, recomendo a Levada do Rei.
variants.translation: For a good trail, I recommend Levada do Rei.
variants.note: Reuses 'trilho' vocabulary.

variants.text: Para ver uma cascata, recomendo a do Risco.
variants.translation: To see a waterfall, I recommend Risco's.
variants.note: Reuses 'cascata' vocabulary.

variants.text: Não te esqueças dos ténis de caminhada.
É bom levar um casaco, pode ficar fresco.
variants.translation: It's good to bring a jacket, it can get cool.

phrase_patterns.base: Em maio as flores estão em pleno.

variants.text: Em agosto as cascatas estão com mais água.
variants.translation: In August the waterfalls have more water.

variants.text: Em junho os trilhos são mais verdes.
variants.translation: In June the trails are greener.

variants.text: Em setembro o tempo está ótimo para banhos de sol.
variants.translation: In September the weather is great for sunbathing.

dialogues.title: Beach Recommendations
dialogues.context: A tourist asks a local for beach recommendations in Madeira.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: Olá! Queria ir à praia. Qual recomendas?
lines.translation: Hello! I wanted to go to the beach. Which one do you recommend?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Pois, para praia de areia, recomendo Calheta. É muito boa.
lines.translation: Well, for a sand beach, I recommend Calheta. It's very good.

lines.speaker: Turista
lines.text: E para praia de seixo? Há alguma boa?
lines.translation: And for a pebble beach? Is there a good one?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Sim, a Formosa, perto do Funchal. Ou a do Seixal, no norte.
lines.translation: Yes, Formosa, near Funchal. Or Seixal's, in the north.

dialogues.title: Hiking Preparations
dialogues.context: Two friends are planning a hike on a levada.

lines.speaker: Amigo 1
lines.text: Então, vamos fazer aquele trilho da Levada do Alecrim amanhã?
lines.translation: So, are we doing that Levada do Alecrim trail tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Amigo 2
lines.text: Pois, sim! Mas leva sempre capa de chuva, o tempo muda rápido na serra.
lines.translation: Yes, sure! But always bring a rain jacket, the weather changes fast in the mountains.

lines.speaker: Amigo 1
lines.text: Ah, boa ideia! E os ténis de caminhada, claro.
lines.translation: Ah, good idea! And hiking shoes, of course.

lines.speaker: Amigo 2
lines.text: Isso mesmo! E água. Vamos ver uma cascata bonita lá.
lines.translation: That's right! And water. We're going to see a beautiful waterfall there.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local information desk asking about a good hiking trail.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! Can I help you?

options.text: Sim, queria saber de um bom trilho.
options.translation: Yes, I wanted to know about a good trail.
options.feedback: Good opening! You clearly state your need.

options.text: Onde posso encontrar uma praia de areia?
options.translation: Where can I find a sand beach?
options.feedback: You asked about a beach, but the scenario is about hiking. Try to stick to the topic.

nodes.npc_text: Para um trilho fácil e bonito, recomendo a Levada do Alecrim. Tem uma cascata no fim. É para amanhã?
nodes.npc_translation: For an easy and beautiful trail, I recommend Levada do Alecrim. It has a waterfall at the end. Is it for tomorrow?

options.text: Sim, é para amanhã. O que devo levar?
options.translation: Yes, it's for tomorrow. What should I bring?
options.feedback: Excellent, you confirmed and asked for important details.

options.text: Não, talvez noutro dia. Há mais cascatas?
options.translation: No, maybe another day. Are there more waterfalls?
options.feedback: You changed the plan but still asked a relevant question.

nodes.npc_text: Para praia de areia, recomendo Calheta. É muito popular. Quer saber mais sobre trilhos?
nodes.npc_translation: For a sand beach, I recommend Calheta. It's very popular. Do you want to know more about trails?

options.text: Sim, por favor. Queria mesmo um trilho.
options.translation: Yes, please. I really wanted a trail.
options.feedback: Good recovery, you redirected to the scenario's topic.

nodes.npc_text: Leva sempre capa de chuva e ténis de caminhada. E água, claro. O tempo pode mudar rápido na serra.
nodes.npc_translation: Always bring a rain jacket and hiking shoes. And water, of course. The weather can change quickly in the mountains.

options.text: Pois é, obrigado pelas dicas!
options.translation: That's right, thanks for the tips!
options.feedback: You acknowledged the advice and thanked them. Good ending.

options.text: Está bem. E onde fica o início do trilho?
options.translation: Okay. And where is the start of the trail?
options.feedback: You asked for more practical information. Very good!

nodes.npc_text: Sim, há muitas. A do Risco é famosa, mas o trilho é um bocado mais longo. Para ver muitas cascatas, o melhor é ir no inverno ou primavera.
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, there are many. Risco's is famous, but the trail is a bit longer. To see many waterfalls, it's best to go in winter or spring.

options.text: Obrigado pela informação!
options.translation: Thanks for the information!
options.feedback: You thanked the person. Good ending.

nodes.npc_text: O início é na Rabaçal, no concelho da Calheta. Precisa de autocarro ou vai de carro?
nodes.npc_translation: The start is in Rabaçal, in the municipality of Calheta. Do you need a bus or are you going by car?

options.text: Vou de carro. Obrigado!
options.translation: I'm going by car. Thank you!
options.feedback: You provided the information and thanked them. Good ending.

options.text: Preciso de autocarro, por favor.
options.translation: I need a bus, please.
options.feedback: You asked for more details, which is practical. Good ending.

mission.title: Plan a Madeiran Hike
prep: Ask a local for a good hiking trail.
Ask what kind of gear you should bring.
Ask about the best time of year to see waterfalls.
fallback_phrases: Pode falar mais devagar, por favor?

cultural_notes.title: Addressing Locals
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, as in mainland Portugal, it's common to use 'tu' (informal 'you') with people your age or younger, or in casual settings. For older people, shopkeepers, or officials, use 'o senhor' (the gentleman) or 'a senhora' (the lady) with the third-person verb form for politeness. Avoid 'você' as it can sound distant or even rude in some contexts.

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Weather and Hiking
cultural_notes.body: The weather in Madeira can be very unpredictable, especially in the mountains (serra). It's common for it to be sunny on the coast and rainy or foggy inland on the same day. Always check the forecast for the specific area you plan to hike and be prepared with appropriate clothing, including a rain jacket, even if the day starts sunny.

dialogues.title: Leaky Tap
dialogues.context: A resident calls a plumber because of a leaky tap in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Bom dia, é da canalização 'Rápido & Bom'?
lines.translation: Good morning, is this 'Rápido & Bom' plumbing?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Sim, pois não. Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Yes, how can I help you?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Tenho uma torneira a pingar na cozinha. Preciso de um canalizador.
lines.translation: I have a tap dripping in the kitchen. I need a plumber.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Ah, pois. Uma torneira a pingar. É urgente? Temos um técnico livre p'ra amanhã de manhã.
lines.translation: Ah, right. A dripping tap. Is it urgent? We have a technician free for tomorrow morning.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Amanhã de manhã está ótimo. É possível entre as nove e as dez?
lines.translation: Tomorrow morning is great. Is it possible between nine and ten?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Sim, posso agendar. Qual é a sua morada e o seu nome, por favor?
lines.translation: Yes, I can schedule that. What is your address and name, please?

dialogues.title: Missing Delivery
dialogues.context: A neighbour asks about a package, but it seems to be missing.

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Bom dia, Inês. Recebeste alguma encomenda p'ra mim?
lines.translation: Good morning, Inês. Did you receive any package for me?

lines.speaker: Inês
lines.text: Bom dia, Dona Maria! Não, não recebi nada. Porquê?
lines.translation: Good morning, Dona Maria! No, I didn't receive anything. Why?

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Pois é que a transportadora diz que entregou ontem. Era um livro.
lines.translation: Well, the delivery company says they delivered it yesterday. It was a book.

lines.speaker: Inês
lines.text: Ontem? Não vi ninguém da transportadora. Estive cá o dia todo. Que estranho!
lines.translation: Yesterday? I didn't see anyone from the delivery company. I was here all day. How strange!

lines.speaker: Vizinha
lines.text: Pois é. Vou ter de ligar p'ra eles. Obrigada na mesma, querida.
lines.translation: Indeed. I'll have to call them. Thanks anyway, dear.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling the electricity company because your last bill is much higher than usual, and you think there's a mistake. You need to explain the problem and ask them to check it.

nodes.npc_text: Boa tarde, EDP. Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good afternoon, EDP. How can I help you?

options.text: Boa tarde. A minha fatura da eletricidade está muito alta.
options.translation: Good afternoon. My electricity bill is very high.
options.feedback: Good start! You clearly state the problem.

options.text: Olá. Queria falar sobre a minha conta.
options.translation: Hello. I wanted to talk about my account.
options.feedback: A bit vague, but acceptable. Try to be more specific next time.

nodes.npc_text: Compreendo. Qual é o seu número de cliente, por favor?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. What is your client number, please?

options.feedback: Direct and correct. Now explain the issue with the bill.

options.text: O meu número de cliente está na fatura. É 123456789.
options.translation: My client number is on the bill. It's 123456789.
options.feedback: Excellent, you're giving the information clearly.

nodes.npc_text: Obrigada. Pode explicar qual é o problema com a fatura?
nodes.npc_translation: Thank you. Can you explain what the problem is with the bill?

options.text: Sim. Normalmente pago 30 euros, mas esta fatura é de 80 euros. Acho que há um erro.
options.translation: Yes. I usually pay 30 euros, but this bill is 80 euros. I think there's a mistake.
options.feedback: Very clear explanation, comparing with your usual bill.

options.text: É muito mais alta do que o normal. Não percebo porquê.
options.translation: It's much higher than normal. I don't understand why.
options.feedback: Good, but try to be more specific if you can (e.g., give numbers).

nodes.npc_text: Certo. Vou verificar aqui no sistema. Um momento, por favor.
nodes.npc_translation: Okay. I'll check here in the system. One moment, please.

options.text: Pois não. Obrigada.
options.translation: Of course. Thank you.
options.feedback: Polite and appropriate response while waiting.

options.text: Está bem.
options.translation: Okay.
options.feedback: Simple and correct.

nodes.npc_text: Senhor/a, parece que houve uma leitura estimada que foi muito alta. Posso fazer um acerto ou enviar um técnico para verificar o contador.
nodes.npc_translation: Sir/Madam, it seems there was an estimated reading that was too high. I can make an adjustment or send a technician to check the meter.

options.text: Por favor, pode fazer o acerto. Não preciso de um técnico.
options.translation: Please, can you make the adjustment. I don't need a technician.
options.feedback: Good, you chose the simpler solution.

options.text: Prefiro que um técnico venha verificar o contador, por favor.
options.translation: I prefer that a technician comes to check the meter, please.
options.feedback: Good, you're asking for a more thorough check.

mission.title: Call the electricity company about an incorrect bill

Note down the amount of the current bill and your usual average bill.
Be ready to explain why you think the bill is wrong.
fallback_phrases: Não percebi, pode repetir, por favor?

Qual é o meu número de cliente?
likely_responses: Bom dia/Boa tarde, em que posso ajudar?
Qual é o seu número de cliente?
Qual é o problema com a fatura?

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Service Interactions
cultural_notes.body: When interacting with service staff (e.g., plumbers, utility company representatives), it's customary to use polite forms like 'por favor' (please) and 'obrigado/a' (thank you). Even when expressing dissatisfaction, maintaining a polite tone is important for a smooth interaction. Avoid overly direct or demanding language.

cultural_notes.title: 'Pois Não' - A Versatile Response
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, 'Pois não' is a very common and versatile phrase, especially in service contexts. It can mean 'Yes, how can I help you?' when answering the phone, or 'Of course' / 'Go ahead' when someone asks for permission or indicates they are about to do something for you. It conveys readiness and willingness to assist.

roleplay.scenario: You need to call a plumber because your kitchen sink is blocked. You need to explain the problem and arrange a time for them to come.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, canalizador Silva, diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, Plumber Silva, speak!

options.text: Bom dia. Chamo-me [o seu nome]. A minha banca da cozinha está entupida.
options.translation: Good morning. My name is [your name]. My kitchen sink is blocked.
options.feedback: Great start, clearly state your name and the issue.

options.text: Olá. Tenho um problema com a água.
options.translation: Hello. I have a problem with the water.
options.feedback: Good attempt, but be more specific about the problem and introduce yourself.

nodes.npc_text: Um problema com a água? Pode ser mais específico, por favor? E como se chama?
nodes.npc_translation: A problem with the water? Can you be more specific, please? And what's your name?

options.text: Sim, a banca da cozinha está entupida. Chamo-me [o seu nome].
options.translation: Yes, the kitchen sink is blocked. My name is [your name].
options.feedback: Good recovery, now the plumber knows what's wrong.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, a banca entupida. Pois, é um problema comum. Onde é a sua morada?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, the blocked sink. Yes, that's a common problem. What's your address?

options.text: É na Rua [nome da rua], número dezasseis.
options.translation: It's on [street name] Street, number sixteen.
options.feedback: Excellent, providing the full address is essential.

nodes.npc_text: Rua [nome da rua], dezasseis. Certo. Quando é que está em casa p'ra eu ir lá?
nodes.npc_translation: [Street name] Street, sixteen. Right. When will you be home for me to go there?

options.text: Posso estar em casa amanhã, depois das catorze horas.
options.translation: I can be home tomorrow, after two PM.
options.feedback: Perfect, giving a clear time frame is very helpful.

options.text: Estou em casa de manhã, às sete.
options.translation: I'm home in the morning, at seven.
options.feedback: You've given a time, but consider if 'seven' is a practical time for a plumber.

nodes.npc_text: Às sete da manhã é um bocado cedo. Pode ser amanhã às nove e meia?
nodes.npc_translation: Seven in the morning is a bit early. Can it be tomorrow at half past nine?

options.text: Sim, às nove e meia está ótimo. Obrigado.
options.translation: Yes, half past nine is great. Thank you.
options.feedback: Great, you've confirmed the appointment.

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã, depois das catorze horas. Está bem. Eu vou lá. Obrigado.
nodes.npc_translation: Tomorrow, after two PM. Okay. I'll go there. Thank you.

options.text: Obrigado eu. Até amanhã.
options.translation: Thank you. See you tomorrow.
options.feedback: Excellent, you've successfully arranged the visit!

cultural_notes.title: Politeness in Service Interactions
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, politeness is highly valued in service interactions. Always use 'por favor' or 'se faz favor' when asking for something. When addressing service staff or officials, 'o senhor'/'a senhora' (sir/madam) is generally preferred over 'você' to show respect, especially if you don't know the person. A simple 'bom dia' or 'boa tarde' to start the conversation is also crucial.

cultural_notes.title: The Versatility of 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'pois é' are very common interjections in European Portuguese, especially in Madeira. They can mean 'yes', 'indeed', 'that's right', 'of course', or simply acknowledge what the other person has said. It's a natural filler that helps conversations flow and shows you are listening, but it's not a direct translation of 'yes' in all contexts.

roleplay.scenario: You are calling a local handyman to fix a leaking tap in your kitchen. You need to explain the problem and arrange a time.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia, 'O Faz Tudo', diga!
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning, 'The Handyman', speak!

options.text: Bom dia. Queria marcar uma reparação, por favor.
options.translation: Good morning. I'd like to schedule a repair, please.
options.feedback: Good start, clear and polite.

options.text: Olá. A minha torneira está a pingar.
options.translation: Hello. My tap is dripping.
options.feedback: You got straight to the point, but 'queria marcar uma reparação' is more direct for scheduling.

nodes.npc_text: Claro. Qual é o problema?
nodes.npc_translation: Of course. What's the problem?

options.text: A torneira da cozinha está a pingar. Já estava assim há uns dias.
options.translation: The kitchen tap is dripping. It's been like this for a few days.
options.feedback: Excellent, you used 'estava' for a past ongoing state.

options.text: A torneira da cozinha pingou e continua a pingar.
options.translation: The kitchen tap dripped and continues to drip.
options.feedback: Good, but 'já estava assim há uns dias' sounds more natural for an ongoing issue.

nodes.npc_text: Percebo. Quando estaria disponível para eu ir lá ver?
nodes.npc_translation: I understand. When would you be available for me to go there and see?

options.text: Eu estaria em casa amanhã de manhã.
options.translation: I would be home tomorrow morning.
options.feedback: Perfect use of the conditional for hypothetical availability.

options.text: Posso estar em casa amanhã de manhã.
options.translation: I can be home tomorrow morning.
options.feedback: This works, but 'estaria' directly answers his conditional question.

nodes.npc_text: Amanhã de manhã, então. Estive a pensar que talvez às 9h seria bom. Serve-lhe?
nodes.npc_translation: Tomorrow morning, then. I was thinking that maybe 9 am would be good. Does that suit you?

options.text: Sim, às 9h estaria ótimo. Muito obrigado!
options.translation: Yes, 9 am would be great. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: Excellent, polite and confirms the time.

options.text: Pois, às 9h está bom.
options.translation: Yes, 9 am is good.
options.feedback: Good, but 'estaria ótimo' is a bit more enthusiastic and reinforces the conditional tense.

mission.title: Agendar uma Limpeza

Relembre-se de como explicaria o problema usando diferentes tempos verbais.

cultural_notes.title: The Versatility of 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'pois é' are extremely common interjections in European Portuguese, especially in Madeira. They can mean 'yes', 'indeed', 'that's right', 'of course', or simply acknowledge what the other person said, often showing agreement or understanding. It's a natural filler that helps conversations flow.

cultural_notes.title: Tense for Hypotheticals: The Conditional
cultural_notes.body: The conditional tense ('estaria', 'gostaria') is frequently used in Portuguese for polite requests, hypothetical situations, or to express what 'would' happen. It's a key tense for sounding natural when making plans or discussing possibilities, as seen in the roleplay when asking about availability.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local café in Câmara de Lobos. You need to order a coffee from the barista, and then you see an old friend, Miguel, at another table.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Em que posso ajudar?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! How can I help?

options.text: Bom dia, queria uma bica, por favor.
options.translation: Good morning, I'd like an espresso, please.
options.feedback: Correct! You used the polite 'queria' and 'bica' (espresso in Portugal).

options.text: Olá! Dá-me um café!
options.translation: Hi! Give me a coffee!
options.feedback: This is too informal and direct for a service interaction. Try to be more polite.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, aqui está. Mais alguma coisa? Ah, o Miguel está ali, se o quiser cumprimentar.
nodes.npc_translation: Here you go. Anything else? Oh, Miguel is over there, if you want to greet him.

options.text: Obrigado! Olá Miguel! Como estás?
options.translation: Thanks! Hi Miguel! How are you?
options.feedback: Excellent! You switched to the informal 'tu' for your friend.

options.text: Obrigado! Olá Miguel! Como está?
options.translation: Thanks! Hi Miguel! How are you?
options.feedback: Miguel is your old friend. 'Como está?' is too formal here. Try using 'tu'.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Tudo bem, e tu? Há quanto tempo!
nodes.npc_translation: Hi! All good, and you? Long time no see!

options.text: Pois é! Tudo ótimo, obrigado!
options.translation: That's right! All great, thanks!
options.feedback: Perfect! You've navigated both registers successfully.

mission.title: Order a coffee and greet a friend
prep: Practice ordering a coffee politely using 'queria' and 'por favor'.
Rehearse informal greetings like 'Olá, como estás?' for friends.
Think about when to use 'o senhor/a senhora' (for service people) and 'tu' (for friends).

cultural_notes.title: The Nuance of 'Você' in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: While 'você' technically means 'you (formal)', in Portugal, it's often avoided in direct address. Using 'o senhor' (for men) or 'a senhora' (for women) is the standard polite way to address strangers, elders, or people in service roles. 'Você' can sometimes sound distancing or even slightly impolite, especially if used with someone you don't know well. Stick to 'o senhor/a senhora' for politeness, or simply use the third person verb conjugation without the pronoun (e.g., 'Como está?' instead of 'Como está você?').

cultural_notes.title: Plural Address: 'Vocês' vs. 'Os senhores/As senhoras'
cultural_notes.body: When addressing a group, 'vocês' is the common plural 'you'. It's generally neutral and can be used in most situations. However, in very formal contexts, or when addressing a group of respected individuals, 'os senhores' (for men or mixed groups) or 'as senhoras' (for women) might be used for heightened politeness, though this is less common in everyday interactions compared to the singular forms.

dialogues.title: Calling a Plumber
dialogues.context: A customer calls a plumbing service because of a leak in their kitchen.

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Bom dia, é da canalização?
lines.translation: Good morning, is this the plumbing service?

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Bom dia, sim, diga! Em que posso ajudar?
lines.translation: Good morning, yes, speak! How can I help?

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima
lines.text: Preciso de um canalizador, tenho uma fuga de água na cozinha.
lines.translation: I need a plumber, I have a water leak in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Canalizador
lines.text: Pois, percebo. Onde é a sua morada?
lines.translation: Right, I understand. What's your address?

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima

lines.speaker: Dona Fátima

lines.speaker: Canalizador

dialogues.title: Discussing a Career Change
dialogues.context: Two friends/neighbours in Madeira discuss a recent life change over coffee.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Então, Mário, como é que tem corrido o novo trabalho?

lines.speaker: Mário
lines.text: Ah, Sofia, muito melhor! Decidi mudar de vida, sabes? O trabalho anterior dava-me esgotamento.
lines.translation: Ah, Sofia, much better! I decided to change my life, you know? The previous job gave me burnout.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Pois, imagino. E agora, o que é que fazes?
lines.translation: Right, I can imagine. And now, what do you do?

lines.speaker: Mário
lines.text: Agora trabalho por conta própria, faço consultoria para pequenas empresas. É o meu objectivo, ter mais liberdade.
lines.translation: Now I work for myself, I do consultancy for small businesses. It's my goal to have more freedom.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Que bom! Fico contente por ti.
lines.translation: How good! I'm happy for you.

lines.speaker: Mário
lines.text: Obrigado! É um bocado assustador, mas estou a gostar imenso.

roleplay.scenario: You need to call a cleaning service for your apartment in Funchal.

options.text: Olá, queria saber os vossos preços.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. É uma limpeza regular ou uma limpeza mais profunda? E onde é a morada?

options.text: É uma limpeza regular, na Rua da Levada, número dez.

options.translation: I didn't understand. Can you repeat?

nodes.npc_text: Os preços dependem do tipo de limpeza e do tamanho do apartamento. Quantos quartos tem?
nodes.npc_translation: Prices depend on the type of cleaning and the size of the apartment. How many bedrooms does it have?

options.text: É um T2, dois quartos.
options.translation: It's a two-bedroom apartment, two bedrooms.
options.feedback: Good, 'T2' is a common way to refer to a two-bedroom apartment.

options.text: Não sei. Pode dar-me uma estimativa?
options.translation: I don't know. Can you give me an estimate?
options.feedback: Asking for an estimate is a good way to get an idea of the cost.

nodes.npc_text: Para um T2, uma limpeza regular custa cerca de quarenta euros. Uma limpeza profunda é mais cara.
nodes.npc_translation: For a two-bedroom apartment, a regular cleaning costs about forty euros. A deep cleaning is more expensive.

options.text: Certo, quero marcar uma limpeza regular.
options.translation: Okay, I want to book a regular cleaning.
options.feedback: You've decided on the service and are ready to book.

options.text: Obrigado/a, vou pensar e ligo mais tarde.
options.translation: Thank you, I'll think about it and call later.
options.feedback: It's fine to take time to decide. Remember to say 'obrigado' if you're male, 'obrigada' if female.

nodes.npc_text: Muito bem. Temos disponibilidade para a próxima terça-feira de manhã, às nove. Serve-lhe?
nodes.npc_translation: Very well. We have availability for next Tuesday morning, at nine. Does that suit you?

options.text: Sim, terça-feira às nove está bem.
options.translation: Yes, Tuesday at nine is fine.
options.feedback: You confirmed the appointment time.

options.text: Não, prefiro na quarta-feira.
options.translation: No, I prefer Wednesday.
options.feedback: It's good to state your preference if the first offer doesn't work.

nodes.npc_text: Quarta-feira, sim. Às dez da manhã?
nodes.npc_translation: Wednesday, yes. At ten in the morning?

options.text: Sim, perfeito.
options.translation: Yes, perfect.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've found a suitable time.

options.text: Não, prefiro à tarde.
options.translation: No, I prefer in the afternoon.
options.feedback: Keep negotiating until you find a time that works for you.

nodes.npc_text: À tarde na quarta-feira, temos às duas. É a nossa última vaga para essa semana.
nodes.npc_translation: Wednesday afternoon, we have at two. It's our last slot for that week.

options.text: Está ótimo, às duas na quarta.
options.translation: That's great, at two on Wednesday.
options.feedback: You've accepted the final offer. Good job!

options.text: Não, obrigado/a. Vou procurar noutro sítio.
options.translation: No, thank you. I'll look elsewhere.
options.feedback: It's okay to decline if the options don't suit you.

nodes.npc_text: Confirmado então! A limpeza será na sua morada. Alguma questão?
nodes.npc_translation: Confirmed then! The cleaning will be at your address. Any questions?

options.text: Não, está tudo bem. Obrigado/a.
options.translation: No, everything is fine. Thank you.
options.feedback: You've successfully booked the service!

options.text: Quanto tempo demora a limpeza?
options.translation: How long does the cleaning take?
options.feedback: It's good to ask about the duration.

nodes.npc_text: Para uma limpeza regular de um T2, cerca de duas a três horas.
nodes.npc_translation: For a regular cleaning of a two-bedroom apartment, about two to three hours.

options.text: Certo. Muito obrigado/a!
options.translation: Okay. Thank you very much!
options.feedback: You have all the information you need. Well done!

mission.title: Scheduling a Cleaning Service
prep: Practice asking for a cleaning service for your apartment.
Be ready to provide your address and preferred time/day.
Ask about the cost of the service.
likely_responses: Quando é que gostaria da limpeza?
É uma limpeza regular ou profunda?

cultural_notes.title: Answering the Phone in Portugal
cultural_notes.body: When answering the phone in Portugal, especially for businesses, you'll often hear 'Diga!' (Speak!) or 'Estou!' (I am!). As a caller, you typically start with 'Bom dia'/'Boa tarde' and state your purpose directly, often using a polite address like 'o senhor' or 'a senhora' if you don't know the person.

cultural_notes.title: Politeness with Service Workers
cultural_notes.body: When speaking to service workers (e.g., cleaners, plumbers, shop assistants), it is common and polite to use 'o senhor' / 'a senhora' even if they don't use it with you. This shows respect and maintains a formal-polite register. Avoid 'você' as a default polite address in these contexts, as it can sometimes sound distancing or overly direct in Portugal.

situations.title: Madeira in Depth
situations.summary: Speak about Madeira with genuine local knowledge and emotional resonance.

variants.text: A floresta laurissilva é Património Mundial.
variants.translation: The Laurisilva forest is a World Heritage Site.

variants.text: A Laurissilva é única no mundo, sabes?
variants.translation: The Laurisilva is unique in the world, you know?

variants.text: É um tesouro, a nossa floresta laurissilva.
variants.translation: It's a treasure, our Laurisilva forest.

phrase_patterns.base: O Pico é de cortar a respiração.

variants.text: A vista do Pico é de cortar a respiração, sim!
variants.translation: The view from Pico is breathtaking, yes!

variants.text: A paisagem lá em cima é de cortar a respiração.
variants.translation: The landscape up there is breathtaking.

variants.text: Uhm, a beleza do Pico é de cortar a respiração!
variants.translation: Uhm, the beauty of Pico is breathtaking!

variants.text: Fiquei sem palavras com tanta beleza!
variants.translation: I was speechless with so much beauty!

variants.text: Pois é, fiquei sem palavras com o que vi.
variants.translation: That's right, I was speechless with what I saw.

variants.text: Com aquela paisagem, fiquei sem palavras.
variants.translation: With that landscape, I was speechless.

dialogues.title: Memories of Madeira
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Maria and João, are chatting about their favourite spots on the island.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Então, João, estiveste no Pico outro dia, não foi?
lines.translation: So, João, you were at Pico the other day, weren't you?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Pois, Maria! E a vista do Pico é mesmo de cortar a respiração, sabes?
lines.translation: Indeed, Maria! And the view from Pico is really breathtaking, you know?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Ah, pois é! Fiquei sem palavras da última vez que lá fui.
lines.translation: Oh, that's right! I was speechless the last time I went there.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: É inesquecível, não é? E a Laurissilva... é UNESCO, uma maravilha.
lines.translation: It's unforgettable, isn't it? And the Laurisilva... it's UNESCO, a marvel.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Um orgulho para o nosso arquipélago, sem dúvida.
lines.translation: A pride for our archipelago, no doubt.

roleplay.scenario: You are at a local café in Funchal, and the owner, a friendly Madeiran, asks you about your favourite places on the island.

nodes.npc_text: Então, e tu? O que é que mais gostas aqui na Madeira? Já tens algum sítio preferido?
nodes.npc_translation: So, what about you? What do you like most here in Madeira? Do you already have a favourite place?

options.text: Gosto muito da floresta laurissilva.
options.translation: I really like the Laurisilva forest.
options.feedback: Good start! You expressed a preference clearly.

options.text: A vista do Pico é de cortar a respiração.
options.translation: The view from Pico is breathtaking.
options.feedback: Excellent use of the phrase 'de cortar a respiração'!

options.text: Sinto muita saudade quando não estou cá.
options.translation: I feel a lot of longing when I'm not here.
options.feedback: Nice, you used 'saudade' to express your feeling for the island.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, a Laurissilva! É mesmo especial, não é? É Património Mundial, sabes. O que é que te atrai mais nela?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, the Laurisilva! It's really special, isn't it? It's a World Heritage Site, you know. What attracts you most to it?

options.text: É uma floresta única no mundo.
options.translation: It's a unique forest in the world.
options.feedback: You reinforced its uniqueness effectively.

options.text: Gosto da paz e do verde.
options.translation: I like the peace and the green.
options.feedback: Simple and clear, expressing your personal connection.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, o Pico! É inesquecível. Fiquei sem palavras da última vez que subi. Já foste lá muitas vezes?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, Pico! It's unforgettable. I was speechless the last time I went up. Have you been there many times?

options.text: Ainda não, mas quero voltar.
options.translation: Not yet, but I want to go back.
options.feedback: A natural and honest response.

options.text: Sim, e cada vez é melhor.
options.translation: Yes, and each time it's better.
options.feedback: You conveyed enthusiasm well.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, a saudade... Essa é uma palavra bem portuguesa! É bom saber que a Madeira te toca assim. O que é que te faz sentir mais saudade?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, 'saudade'... That's a very Portuguese word! It's good to know Madeira touches you like that. What makes you feel the most 'saudade'?

options.text: Das pessoas e da comida.
options.translation: Of the people and the food.
options.feedback: You identified specific aspects, making your answer more personal.

options.text: Da beleza do arquipélago.
options.translation: Of the beauty of the archipelago.
options.feedback: Good use of 'arquipélago' and a general appreciation.

nodes.npc_text: É verdade, a Laurissilva é mesmo um sítio para relaxar e apreciar a natureza. Fico contente que gostes tanto!
nodes.npc_translation: It's true, the Laurisilva is really a place to relax and appreciate nature. I'm glad you like it so much!

nodes.npc_text: Sim, é um lugar que nos faz sentir pequenos perante tanta grandeza. Voltar é sempre bom. Obrigado!
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, it's a place that makes us feel small before such grandeur. Going back is always good. Thank you!

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, a Madeira tem esse efeito nas pessoas. É um prazer ter-te por cá. Volta sempre!
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, Madeira has that effect on people. It's a pleasure to have you here. Come back always!

mission.title: Share Your Madeiran Love
prep: Think about your absolute favourite spot or experience in Madeira.
Practice describing it using phrases like 'é de cortar a respiração' or 'é inesquecível'.
Prepare to explain *why* it's your favourite, perhaps mentioning its unique beauty or feeling.
likely_responses: Ah, sim, esse sítio é lindo!
Nunca lá fui, mas quero ir.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Madeiran Pride
cultural_notes.body: Locals in Madeira often have a strong sense of pride in their island's natural beauty and unique culture. When you express genuine admiration for places like the Laurisilva forest or Pico do Areeiro, it's often met with warmth and agreement. Using phrases like 'Património Mundial' or 'única no mundo' shows you appreciate the island's special status.

cultural_notes.title: The Nuance of 'Pois' and 'Pois é'
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois' and 'Pois é' are very common interjections in European Portuguese, especially in informal conversation. They don't always translate directly but often convey agreement, understanding, or a simple 'indeed'/'that's right'. In Madeira, they are frequently used to acknowledge what someone has said or to show you're following the conversation, adding to the natural flow of dialogue.

situations.title: Complex Structures
situations.summary: Eliminate hesitation from relative clauses, temporal chains, and subjunctives.

phrase_patterns.base: É uma ilha que me apaixonou.

variants.text: É uma cidade que me cativou.
variants.translation: It's a city that captivated me.

variants.text: É um lugar que me surpreendeu imenso.
variants.translation: It's a place that surprised me a lot.

variants.text: Foi uma paisagem que me encantou.
variants.translation: It was a landscape that enchanted me.

variants.text: Desde que cheguei, a vida é diferente.
variants.translation: Since I arrived, life is different.

variants.text: Desde que me mudei para cá, tudo é melhor.
variants.translation: Since I moved here, everything is better.

variants.text: Desde que estou aqui, sinto-me em casa.
variants.translation: Since I've been here, I feel at home.

variants.text: Quando puder vir, vai gostar imenso.
variants.translation: When you can come, you'll like it a lot.

variants.text: Quando visitar, vai ficar encantado com a ilha.
variants.translation: When you visit, you'll be enchanted by the island.

variants.text: Quando tiver tempo, vai querer voltar, de certeza.
variants.translation: When you have time, you'll want to come back, for sure.

dialogues.title: Plans for a friend's visit
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Maria and João, are chatting outside their homes in Madeira about a friend coming to visit.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Olá, João! Então, como é que vai? A sua amiga vem para a semana, certo?
lines.translation: Hello, João! So, how are you? Your friend is coming next week, right?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Olá, Maria! Pois é, vem sim. Estou a planear o que fazer enquanto ela estiver cá.
lines.translation: Hello, Maria! That's right, she is. I'm planning what to do while she's here.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Ah, que bom! E já sabe onde a vai levar? É uma ilha que tem tanto para ver!
lines.translation: Oh, that's good! And do you know where you'll take her? It's an island that has so much to see!

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Sim, quando ela vier, quero levá-la às levadas. Ela gosta muito de natureza, sabe?
lines.translation: Yes, when she comes, I want to take her to the levadas. She really likes nature, you know?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Pois, é uma excelente ideia! Tenho a certeza que ela vai adorar, onde quer que a leve.
lines.translation: That's a great idea! I'm sure she'll love it, wherever you take her.

roleplay.scenario: Your neighbour, Dona Laura, is asking about your plans for the weekend, especially since you mentioned you wanted to explore more of the island.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Então, como é que vai? Já tem planos para o fim de semana? Dizia que queria conhecer mais da ilha.
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! So, how are you? Do you have plans for the weekend? You said you wanted to get to know more of the island.

options.text: Bom dia, Dona Laura! Sim, estou a pensar que, enquanto o tempo estiver bom, vou à Ponta de São Lourenço.
options.translation: Good morning, Dona Laura! Yes, I'm thinking that, while the weather is good, I'll go to Ponta de São Lourenço.
options.feedback: Great use of 'enquanto' for a temporal clause!

options.text: Olá, Dona Laura! Ainda não tenho nada planeado, mas queria ir a um sítio que nunca visitei.
options.translation: Hello, Dona Laura! I don't have anything planned yet, but I wanted to go to a place I've never visited.
options.feedback: Good use of a relative clause. Now try to add a temporal element or a subjunctive.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, a Ponta de São Lourenço! É lindo lá. Quando lá chegar, vai ver paisagens incríveis. E depois?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, Ponta de São Lourenço! It's beautiful there. When you get there, you'll see incredible landscapes. And then?

options.text: Depois, se o tempo permitir, quero ir a um restaurante onde sirvam bolo do caco fresco.
options.translation: Afterwards, if the weather permits, I want to go to a restaurant where they serve fresh bolo do caco.
options.feedback: Excellent! You used 'se' for a conditional, and 'onde' with a subjunctive verb for a specific kind of place.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é. Há tantos sítios bons. Se eu fosse a si, ia a um sítio que tivesse uma boa vista para o mar. O que acha?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right. There are so many good places. If I were you, I'd go to a place that had a good sea view. What do you think?

options.text: Sim, é uma boa ideia. Talvez vá à Ribeira Brava, onde o pôr do sol é maravilhoso.
options.translation: Yes, that's a good idea. Maybe I'll go to Ribeira Brava, where the sunset is wonderful.
options.feedback: Very good! You've used 'onde' to specify a location with a descriptive clause.

nodes.npc_text: Hummm, bolo do caco! Que delícia! Espero que encontre um bom. E depois, quando voltar, diga-me como correu a sua aventura.
nodes.npc_translation: Mmm, bolo do caco! How delicious! I hope you find a good one. And then, when you come back, tell me how your adventure went.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, sim! A Ribeira Brava é linda. Tenho a certeza que vai gostar. Aproveite bem!
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, yes! Ribeira Brava is beautiful. I'm sure you'll like it. Enjoy!

mission.title: Describe a favourite Madeiran place
prep: Think of your favourite place in Madeira.
Consider what makes it special and why you like it.
Prepare to use 'que' or 'onde' to describe it.
Think about when you first visited it, using 'desde que' or 'quando'.
fallback_phrases: É um sítio que adoro, mas é difícil de descrever.
Não sei bem como explicar, mas é um lugar especial para mim.
likely_responses: Ah, sim, esse sítio é lindo!
Nunca lá fui, mas parece ser muito bom.
Pois, é um dos meus preferidos também.

cultural_notes.title: The versatility of 'Pois'
cultural_notes.body: In Portuguese, especially in Portugal and Madeira, 'pois' (and 'pois é') is a very common interjection. It can mean 'yes', 'indeed', 'of course', 'well', or simply serve as a filler to acknowledge what someone has said, indicating you've heard them and are processing. It's often used to agree or confirm, or to start a response, making conversations flow more naturally. It's less of a direct translation and more of a conversational lubricant.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Future Plans with 'Quando' + Subjunctive
cultural_notes.body: When discussing future events that are not yet certain or are conditional, Portuguese often uses 'quando' (when) followed by the future subjunctive. This is a key structure at this level. For example, 'Quando ele vier' (When he comes) implies the coming is a future event, rather than a certainty or a habitual action. This contrasts with 'Quando ele vem' (When he comes, as in, regularly or certainly).

situations.title: The Grand Stress Test
situations.summary: Months 1 & 2 Combined. 10-minute unscripted conversation on any topic.

dialogues.title: Weekend Plans
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Maria and João, chat on the street about their weekend plans.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Olá, João! Então, como é que tá tudo? Muito trabalho?
lines.translation: Hello, João! So, how is everything? A lot of work?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Olá, Maria! Pois, tá um bocado. Mas este fim de semana vou p'ra serra, p'ra relaxar.
lines.translation: Hello, Maria! Well, it's a bit much. But this weekend I'm going to the mountains, to relax.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Ah, que bom! P'ra onde é que vais? Alguma levada?
lines.translation: Oh, that's good! Where are you going? To a levada?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Não sei bem, talvez a do Caldeirão Verde. E a senhora, tem planos?
lines.translation: I don't really know, maybe Caldeirão Verde. And you (polite), do you have plans?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Eu? Não, vou ficar por casa. Talvez vá ao mercado no sábado, comprar umas semilhas.
lines.translation: Me? No, I'll stay home. Maybe I'll go to the market on Saturday, to buy some potatoes.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Pois é, o mercado é sempre bom. Então, até breve!
lines.translation: That's right, the market is always good. So, see you soon!

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Até, João! Cuidado na serra!
lines.translation: Bye, João! Be careful in the mountains!

dialogues.title: A Quick Coffee
dialogues.context: A customer orders a coffee and a snack at a busy café in Funchal.

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Diga, está muito barulho aqui, não é?
lines.translation: Tell me, it's very noisy here, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Pois é, senhor. Hoje tá cheio. Quer um café ou uma bica?
lines.translation: That's right, sir. Today it's full. Do you want a coffee or an espresso?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Uma bica, por favor. E um bolo do caco com manteiga.
lines.translation: An espresso, please. And a bolo do caco with butter.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: P'ra já! Quer ler o jornal?
lines.translation: Coming right up! Do you want to read the newspaper?

lines.speaker: Cliente
lines.text: Não, obrigado. Só a bica.
lines.translation: No, thank you. Just the espresso.

lines.speaker: Empregado
lines.text: Ok, já vem.
lines.translation: Okay, it's coming.

roleplay.scenario: Your neighbour, Dona Fátima, stops you in the street to chat about your recent activities and plans.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Então, como é que tem corrido? Muitas novidades por aí?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! So, how has it been going? Many news around?

options.text: Olá, Dona Fátima! Tudo bem, obrigada. Tenho estado um bocado ocupado/a.
options.translation: Hello, Dona Fátima! All good, thank you. I've been a bit busy.
options.feedback: Great start, acknowledging her and giving a common reason for being busy.

options.text: Olá! Pois, tem sido bom. E a senhora, tudo bem?
options.translation: Hello! Well, it's been good. And you (polite), all good?
options.feedback: Good, you returned the question politely. 'Pois' is a natural interjection.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois é. E o que é que tem feito de interessante? Algum passeio?
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, that's right. And what interesting things have you been doing? Any walks?

options.text: Fui à levada das 25 Fontes no fim de semana, foi lindo!
options.translation: I went to the 25 Fontes levada on the weekend, it was beautiful!
options.feedback: Excellent, you shared a specific, local experience. 'Foi lindo!' adds a nice touch.

options.text: Tenho estado a trabalhar imenso, mas no sábado vou ao mercado comprar fruta.
options.translation: I've been working a lot, but on Saturday I'm going to the market to buy fruit.
options.feedback: Good, you balanced work with a concrete, common plan.

nodes.npc_text: Comigo, tudo na mesma, graças a Deus. Mas o tempo tá a melhorar, não é? Ótimo p'ra dar um passeio.
nodes.npc_translation: With me, same as always, thank God. But the weather is improving, isn't it? Great for a walk.

options.text: Sim, concordo! Gosto muito de ir à beira-mar, é relaxante.
options.translation: Yes, I agree! I really like to go to the seafront, it's relaxing.
options.feedback: Perfect, you agreed and shared a pleasant, common activity.

options.text: Pois é. Estou a planear ir à serra este fim de semana, se o tempo ajudar.
options.translation: That's right. I'm planning to go to the mountains this weekend, if the weather helps.
options.feedback: Good, you acknowledged her point and shared a future plan, adding a condition.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, que bom! É tão importante aproveitar a nossa ilha. Fico contente que esteja a gostar!
nodes.npc_translation: Oh, that's good! It's so important to enjoy our island. I'm happy you're enjoying it!

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, a vida é assim. Mas o mercado é sempre bom, não é? Encontra-se de tudo.
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, life is like that. But the market is always good, isn't it? You find everything there.

mission.title: Chatting with a Neighbour
prep: Think of 2-3 things you've done recently (e.g., 'Fui à levada...', 'Estive a trabalhar imenso...').
Prepare 2-3 plans you have for the near future (e.g., 'Vou ao mercado...', 'Estou a planear ir à serra...').
Practice asking 'E a senhora?' or 'E consigo?' to return the question politely.
fallback_phrases: Não sei bem o que dizer.

cultural_notes.title: Directness in Madeiran Conversation
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, conversations among neighbours can be quite direct and to the point. While politeness is valued, there's often less 'small talk' before getting to the substance. Asking about someone's day, work, or plans is a common and natural way to engage.

cultural_notes.title: Common Conversation Starters
cultural_notes.body: Topics like the weather ('o tempo'), family ('a família'), recent activities ('o que tem feito'), or upcoming plans ('planos para o fim de semana') are very common and safe conversation starters with neighbours or acquaintances. Locals also appreciate when you mention local landmarks or activities like 'levadas' or 'o mercado'.

situations.title: Neighbourhood Small Talk
situations.summary: Chatting with a neighbour on the street: weather, the levada, how things are going — the daily social glue of belonging in a Madeira bairro.

variants.note: A common, more casual greeting.

phrase_patterns.base: Fui dar um passeio na levada de {levada_name}.
phrase_patterns.translation: I went for a walk on the {levada_name} levada.

variants.text: Gosto imenso de caminhar nas levadas.
variants.translation: I really like walking on the levadas.

phrase_patterns.base: Pois é, a vida aqui é {how_life_is}, não é?
phrase_patterns.translation: That's right, life here is {how_life_is}, isn't it?

A traditional Madeiran bread, often served with garlic butter.

Used for agreement or acknowledgement in conversation.

dialogues.title: Morning Chat on the Street
dialogues.context: Dona Lúcia and Marco, neighbours, meet on the street near a small grocery store in the morning.

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: Bom dia, Marco! Tudo bem?
lines.translation: Good morning, Marco! All good?

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Bom dia, Dona Lúcia! Sim, tudo bem, obrigado. E a senhora, como está?
lines.translation: Good morning, Dona Lúcia! Yes, all good, thank you. And you, how are you?

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: Está-se bem, está-se bem. Este solzinho já ajuda, não é? Ontem esteve um bocado esquisito.
lines.translation: I'm well, I'm well. This little sun already helps, doesn't it? Yesterday it was a bit strange.

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Pois é, o tempo aqui muda num instante. A senhora vai às compras?
lines.translation: That's right, the weather here changes in an instant. Are you going shopping?

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: Vou sim, preciso de umas semilhas para o jantar. E o Marco, já foi à levada hoje?
lines.translation: Yes, I am, I need some potatoes for dinner. And Marco, have you been to the levada today?

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Ainda não, mas estou a planear ir à tarde. A de São Jorge é sempre bonita nesta altura.
lines.translation: Not yet, but I'm planning to go this afternoon. The São Jorge one is always beautiful at this time of year.

lines.speaker: Dona Lúcia
lines.text: É verdade. Olhe, tenho de ir, que as semilhas não se compram sozinhas! Até logo, Marco!
lines.translation: It's true. Look, I have to go, the potatoes don't buy themselves! See you later, Marco!

lines.speaker: Marco
lines.text: Até logo, Dona Lúcia! Bom dia!
lines.translation: See you later, Dona Lúcia! Good day!

cultural_notes.title: The Importance of Greetings and Small Talk
cultural_notes.body: In Madeiran communities, it's very common and expected to greet neighbours and engage in brief small talk when you meet them on the street or in local shops. This daily interaction is a vital part of social cohesion and belonging. Even a simple 'Bom dia!' or 'Tudo bem?' followed by a quick comment about the weather or daily plans helps maintain community bonds.

cultural_notes.title: Using 'Pois é' for Agreement
cultural_notes.body: 'Pois é' is a very common expression in European Portuguese, especially in informal settings like neighbourhood chats. It's used to express agreement, acknowledgement, or to confirm something that has just been said. It can be translated as 'that's right', 'indeed', or 'it is so'.

roleplay.scenario: You meet your neighbour, Senhor Manuel, near the local minimarket in the morning. He's a friendly older man who enjoys a quick chat.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Como é que está? Está um dia bonito, não está?
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! How are you? It's a beautiful day, isn't it?

options.text: Bom dia, Senhor Manuel! Estou bem, obrigado. Sim, está um dia ótimo!
options.translation: Good morning, Senhor Manuel! I'm well, thank you. Yes, it's a great day!
options.feedback: Great start! You greeted him back and agreed about the weather politely.

options.text: Olá! Tudo bem. O tempo está bom hoje.
options.translation: Hello! All good. The weather is good today.
options.feedback: Good, a slightly more informal but perfectly acceptable response.

nodes.npc_text: Pois é, um dia bom para ir à levada. Eu já fui, à levada do Rei. E o que vai fazer hoje?
nodes.npc_translation: That's right, a good day to go to the levada. I've already been, to the Levada do Rei. And what are you going to do today?

options.text: Que bom! Eu ainda não fui, mas talvez vá à tarde. Gosto imenso da de São Jorge.
options.translation: How nice! I haven't been yet, but maybe I'll go this afternoon. I really like the São Jorge one.
options.feedback: Excellent, you shared your plans and mentioned a local levada.

options.text: Ah, a levada do Rei é muito bonita! Eu hoje vou só tratar de umas coisas em casa.
options.translation: Ah, the Levada do Rei is very beautiful! Today I'm just going to take care of some things at home.
options.feedback: Good, you acknowledged his activity and shared your own simple plans.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, todas as levadas têm o seu encanto. Olhe, preciso de ir comprar um bolo do caco para o almoço. A gente encontra-se por aí!
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, all the levadas have their charm. Look, I need to go buy a bolo do caco for lunch. We'll see each other around!

options.text: Pois é! Até logo, Senhor Manuel! Bom almoço!
options.translation: That's right! See you later, Senhor Manuel! Have a good lunch!
options.feedback: Perfect! You acknowledged his comment and said a friendly goodbye.

options.text: Sim, concordo. Até logo!
options.translation: Yes, I agree. See you later!
options.feedback: Good, a simple and polite way to end the conversation.

Be ready to mention a simple plan or activity: 'Vou dar um passeio', 'Vou às compras'
Practice using 'Pois é' for agreement.
fallback_phrases: Desculpe, tenho um bocado de pressa.
likely_responses: Pois é, o tempo está ótimo.
Sim, as levadas são sempre bonitas.

goals: Initiate and respond to common greetings with neighbours.
Engage in brief small talk about everyday topics like weather or local activities.
Use culturally appropriate expressions like 'Pois é' and local vocabulary.
Maintain a friendly and polite tone in informal conversations.

situations.summary: Enjoying a Madeiran arraial: buying espetada and poncha, joining the crowd, understanding festival announcements and invitations.

variants.note: Slightly less formal

dialogues.title: Ordering at the Arraial
dialogues.context: At a busy food stall during a Madeiran arraial, a local is ordering from a vendor.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Boa noite! O que vai ser?
lines.translation: Good evening! What will it be?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Boa noite! Queria uma espetada e um bolo do caco, por favor.
lines.translation: Good evening! I'd like a beef skewer and a bolo do caco, please.

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Com ou sem semilhas?
lines.translation: With or without potatoes?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Ah, sem semilhas, por enquanto. E uma poncha, pode ser?
lines.translation: Ah, without potatoes for now. And a poncha, if possible?

lines.speaker: Vendedor
lines.text: Claro! Fica tudo dezasseis euros. É p'ra levar ou come aqui?
lines.translation: Of course! That's sixteen euros. Is it to go or are you eating here?

lines.speaker: Local
lines.text: Não, é p'ra comer aqui, no meio da confusão!
lines.translation: No, it's to eat here, in the middle of the hustle and bustle!

dialogues.title: Enjoying the Arraial
dialogues.context: Two friends, Ana and Rui, are trying to find a spot to eat their food at a crowded arraial. The general ambience is noisy.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Ufa, que confusão! Mas a espetada está ótima, não está?
lines.translation: Phew, what a crowd! But the espetada is great, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Rui
lines.text: Está uma delícia! E esta poncha... humm! Precisava mesmo disto.
lines.translation: It's delicious! And this poncha... mmm! I really needed this.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Pois é. A animação também está boa este ano. Estão a tocar umas músicas fixes.
lines.translation: Indeed. The entertainment is also good this year. They're playing some cool songs.

lines.speaker: Rui
lines.text: Sim, sim! Olha, queres ir ver as barraquinhas de artesanato depois? São por ali.
lines.translation: Yes, yes! Look, do you want to go see the craft stalls later? They're over there.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Diz? Não ouvi bem! As barraquinhas de quê?
lines.translation: What? I didn't hear well! The stalls of what?

lines.speaker: Rui
lines.text: De artesanato! P'ra ver se há alguma coisa gira.
lines.translation: Crafts! To see if there's anything nice.

lines.speaker: Ana
lines.text: Ah, sim! Boa ideia. Mas primeiro, mais um bocado de espetada!
lines.translation: Ah, yes! Good idea. But first, a bit more espetada!

cultural_notes.title: The Madeiran Arraial
cultural_notes.body: Arraiais are traditional religious festivals in Madeira, often celebrating a patron saint. While they have a strong religious component (masses, processions), they are also major social events. Expect lively music, dancing, and, most importantly, abundant food and drink stalls. They are a fantastic way to experience Madeiran culture and gastronomy. The atmosphere is usually very informal and joyful.

cultural_notes.title: Ordering Food and Drink
cultural_notes.body: At an arraial, food and drink are typically ordered at 'barracas' (stalls). It's common to queue up, place your order, pay, and then find a spot to eat, often standing or at shared tables. Don't be shy to ask 'Com licença' to navigate through crowds. Using 'por favor' and 'obrigado/a' is always polite, even in a bustling environment. Madeirans often use 'semilha' for potato instead of 'batata'.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a busy arraial in Madeira, feeling hungry and thirsty. You decide to approach a food stall to order some typical Madeiran food and a drink.

nodes.npc_text: Boa noite! O que vai ser? Temos espetada, bolo do caco, e semilhas fritas!
nodes.npc_translation: Good evening! What will it be? We have espetada, bolo do caco, and fried potatoes!

options.text: Queria uma espetada, por favor.
options.translation: I'd like an espetada, please.
options.feedback: Great start! You clearly asked for the espetada. Now, what about a drink?

options.text: Olá! Quanto é que custa a espetada?
options.translation: Hello! How much does the espetada cost?
options.feedback: Good, you asked about the price first. Now you can decide to order.

nodes.npc_text: A espetada é dez euros. As semilhas são três. E as bebidas, temos poncha e água.
nodes.npc_translation: The espetada is ten euros. The potatoes are three. And for drinks, we have poncha and water.

options.text: Ok, queria uma espetada e uma poncha, por favor.
options.translation: Okay, I'd like an espetada and a poncha, please.
options.feedback: Excellent! You ordered both food and drink.

options.text: Só a espetada, então. E uma água, se faz favor.
options.translation: Just the espetada, then. And a water, please.
options.feedback: Good choice, you opted for water. Now, time to pay.

nodes.npc_text: Certo. E p'ra beber? Uma poncha, talvez?
nodes.npc_translation: Alright. And to drink? A poncha, perhaps?

options.text: Sim, uma poncha, por favor.
options.translation: Yes, a poncha, please.
options.feedback: Perfeito! You've ordered both your meal and a typical Madeiran drink.

options.text: Não, obrigada. Só a espetada.
options.translation: No, thank you. Just the espetada.
options.feedback: That's fine, you politely declined the drink. Now to the payment.

nodes.npc_text: Fica tudo doze euros e cinquenta. É p'ra levar ou come aqui?
nodes.npc_translation: That's twelve euros fifty. Is it to go or are you eating here?

options.text: É p'ra comer aqui, obrigada.
options.translation: It's to eat here, thank you.
options.feedback: Good, you specified you're eating there. Make sure you pay!

options.text: É p'ra levar, por favor.
options.translation: It's to go, please.
options.feedback: Excellent, you chose to take it away. Now, pay up!

nodes.npc_text: Aqui está! Bom apetite!
nodes.npc_translation: Here you go! Enjoy your meal!

options.text: Obrigada! Está muito bom!
options.translation: Thank you! It's very good!
options.feedback: Great, you expressed your appreciation for the food. Well done!

options.text: Obrigada! Com licença, posso passar?
options.translation: Thank you! Excuse me, may I pass?
options.feedback: Perfect! You thanked the vendor and politely asked to navigate the crowd. Mission accomplished!

dialogues.title: A Morning Chat in the Village
dialogues.context: Maria and João, neighbours in a small Madeiran village, meet by chance near the local café. It's a slightly grey morning.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Bom dia, João! Que tempo, não é? Parece que está a chover miúdo.
lines.translation: Good morning, João! What weather, isn't it? It looks like it's drizzling lightly.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Bom dia, Maria! Pois é, mas é da Madeira. Nunca se sabe! Vais tomar uma bica?
lines.translation: Good morning, Maria! Indeed, but it's from Madeira. You never know! Are you going for a coffee?

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Vou sim. Tenho de ir ao mercado depois, mas com esta chuvinha... que maçada!
lines.translation: Yes, I am. I have to go to the market afterwards, but with this drizzle... what a nuisance!

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Ah, não te acanhes! É só uma chuvinha. O mercado está coberto. Eu já lá vou também.
lines.translation: Ah, don't be shy! It's just a little rain. The market is covered. I'm going there too.

lines.speaker: Maria
lines.text: Tens razão. Então, até já no mercado!
lines.translation: You're right. So, see you at the market!

cultural_notes.title: Madeiran Weather Talk: 'Chover Miúdo'
cultural_notes.body: 'Está a chover miúdo' is a very common and specific Madeiran expression for light, fine rain or drizzle. While 'chuviscar' is used across Portugal, 'chover miúdo' is a local favourite. It reflects the island's often unpredictable and varied microclimates.

cultural_notes.title: Encouragement and Hospitality: 'Não te acanhes!'
cultural_notes.body: In Portugal, and especially in Madeira, it's common to encourage guests or friends to feel at home and not hold back, particularly when offering food or drink. 'Não te acanhes!' is a warm, informal way to say 'Don't be shy!' or 'Help yourself!', showing genuine hospitality and a desire for you to relax and enjoy.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a small local market stall in Madeira, looking at some fresh fruit. The vendor notices you and offers you a taste of some local produce.

nodes.npc_text: Bom dia! Gostavas de provar esta anona? É da terra, muito fresquinha.
nodes.npc_translation: Good morning! Would you like to try this custard apple? It's local, very fresh.

options.text: Sim, obrigado/a! Parece deliciosa.
options.translation: Yes, thank you! It looks delicious.
options.feedback: Great! You accepted politely and showed enthusiasm. This is a common and welcome response.

options.text: Não, obrigado/a. Só estou a olhar.
options.translation: No, thank you. I'm just looking.
options.feedback: This is polite, but you missed an opportunity to engage with the local culture and hospitality. Vendors often appreciate you trying their produce.

nodes.npc_text: Então, não te acanhes! É mesmo boa. Diz lá o que achas.
nodes.npc_translation: So, don't be shy! It's really good. Tell me what you think.

options.text: Mmm, é fantástica! Muito doce.
options.translation: Mmm, it's fantastic! Very sweet.
options.feedback: Excellent! You tried the fruit and gave positive feedback, engaging naturally with the vendor.

options.text: Obrigado/a pela provinha.
options.translation: Thank you for the taste.
options.feedback: Good, you thanked them. Giving a short opinion ('muito bom', 'delicioso') would be even more engaging.

nodes.npc_text: Pois, como quiseres. Mas se mudares de ideias, é só dizer!
nodes.npc_translation: Well, as you wish. But if you change your mind, just say so!

options.text: Está bem, obrigado/a!
options.translation: Okay, thank you!
options.feedback: You ended the interaction politely. Next time, consider accepting the offer to engage more with local hospitality.

nodes.npc_text: Ainda bem que gostaste! Queres levar uma?
nodes.npc_translation: Glad you liked it! Do you want to take one?

options.text: Sim, por favor! Vou levar duas.
options.translation: Yes, please! I'll take two.
options.feedback: Perfect! You've successfully engaged, appreciated the local produce, and made a purchase.

options.text: Talvez para a próxima. Obrigado/a!
options.translation: Maybe next time. Thank you!
options.feedback: Polite, but a missed opportunity to buy after enjoying the sample. Vendors appreciate it when you buy after tasting.

situations.title: Month 4 Stress Test
situations.summary: Mixed real-time social conversation: neighbours, festival, opinions and local expressions combined under light pressure.

dialogues.title: Festival Talk
dialogues.context: Two neighbours, Sofia and João, meet by chance at the local market, discussing the upcoming arraial.

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Bom dia, João! Tudo bem? Estás a ver as semilhas?
lines.translation: Good morning, João! All good? Are you looking at the potatoes?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Bom dia, Sofia! Sim, estão com bom aspeto. E tu, já a pensar no arraial do próximo fim de semana?
lines.translation: Good morning, Sofia! Yes, they look good. And you, already thinking about next weekend's festival?

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Pois é! Estou com imensa vontade de ir. A música ao vivo é sempre boa.
lines.translation: Indeed! I'm really looking forward to going. The live music is always good.

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: É verdade. E a poncha, claro! Que tal irmos juntos, se não tiveres planos?
lines.translation: It's true. And the poncha, of course! How about we go together, if you don't have plans?

lines.speaker: Sofia
lines.text: Era ótimo! Eu acho que o arraial é sempre o melhor para animar. Vamos então?
lines.translation: That would be great! I think the festival is always the best to liven things up. Shall we go then?

lines.speaker: João
lines.text: Combinado. Falamos durante a semana. Até já!
lines.translation: It's a plan. We'll talk during the week. See you!

cultural_notes.title: Arraiais: The Heart of Madeiran Community
cultural_notes.body: Arraiais are traditional local festivals, often linked to religious celebrations, but they are also vibrant social events. They typically feature live music, traditional food (like espetadas and bolo do caco), drinks (especially poncha), and a lively atmosphere. They are a cornerstone of community life in Madeira, bringing neighbours together for fun and celebration. Expressing enthusiasm for an arraial is a common way to connect with locals.

cultural_notes.title: Expressing Opinions and Making Plans Informally
cultural_notes.body: In Madeira, especially with neighbours or friends, conversations are often informal. Using 'tu' is standard. Phrases like 'Eu acho que...' (I think that...) are common for sharing opinions. When suggesting plans, 'Que tal irmos...?' (How about we go...?) is a friendly way to invite someone. Interjections like 'Pois é!' (Indeed!/That's right!) are used to show agreement and keep the conversation flowing naturally.

roleplay.scenario: You're at a local café, enjoying a bica. Your neighbour, Maria, approaches you and starts a conversation about the weekend and a local event.

nodes.npc_text: Olá! Tudo bem? A aproveitar a bica? Já a pensar nos planos para o fim de semana?
nodes.npc_translation: Hello! All good? Enjoying your espresso? Already thinking about plans for the weekend?

options.text: Olá, Maria! Sim, está tudo bem. Ainda não pensei muito, e tu?
options.translation: Hello, Maria! Yes, all good. I haven't thought much yet, and you?
options.feedback: Good start! You greeted her and turned the question back to her.

options.text: Olá! Estou um bocado cansado, mas sim, a bica ajuda. Ainda não sei os planos.
options.translation: Hello! I'm a bit tired, but yes, the espresso helps. I don't know the plans yet.
options.feedback: It's fine to mention being tired. You still answered her question about plans.

nodes.npc_text: Eu também não. Mas ouvi dizer que vai haver um arraial pequeno na freguesia vizinha. Que tal irmos lá no sábado à noite?
nodes.npc_translation: Me neither. But I heard there's going to be a small festival in the next parish. How about we go there on Saturday night?

options.text: Um arraial? Que boa ideia! Eu acho que o arraial é sempre divertido.
options.translation: A festival? What a good idea! I think the festival is always fun.
options.feedback: Great! You showed enthusiasm and used a relevant phrase pattern.

options.text: Não sei. Estou um bocado cansado com a confusão dos arraiais.
options.translation: I don't know. I'm a bit tired with the crowds at festivals.
options.feedback: It's okay to express hesitation. Maria might try to convince you.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, pois é! O trabalho cansa. Mas ouvi dizer que vai haver um arraial pequeno na freguesia vizinha. Que tal irmos lá no sábado à noite? Talvez ajude a descontrair.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, indeed! Work is tiring. But I heard there's going to be a small festival in the next parish. How about we go there on Saturday night? Maybe it will help you relax.

options.text: Pois, se calhar. Eu acho que a música ao vivo é boa para esquecer o cansaço.
options.translation: Yes, maybe. I think live music is good for forgetting tiredness.
options.feedback: Good, you're considering it and linking it to a positive aspect.

options.text: Não sei, Maria. Estou mesmo sem vontade de ir a sítios com muita gente.
options.translation: I don't know, Maria. I really don't feel like going to places with many people.
options.feedback: You're politely declining. That's a valid option in real life.

nodes.npc_text: Sim, a música e a poncha! É sempre bom. Então, combinado? Encontramo-nos à entrada às nove?
nodes.npc_translation: Yes, the music and the poncha! It's always good. So, it's a plan? Shall we meet at the entrance at nine?

options.text: Sim, combinado! Até sábado então. Obrigada pelo convite!
options.translation: Yes, it's a plan! See you Saturday then. Thanks for the invitation!
options.feedback: Excellent! You confirmed the plan and thanked her politely. Conversation ends well.

nodes.npc_text: Ah, mas este é mais pequeno, não tem tanta gente como os grandes. E a poncha é caseira! Pensa bem.
nodes.npc_translation: Ah, but this one is smaller, it doesn't have as many people as the big ones. And the poncha is homemade! Think about it.

options.text: Hmm, poncha caseira... Isso já me convence mais. Que tal irmos então?
options.translation: Hmm, homemade poncha... That convinces me more. How about we go then?
options.feedback: You're swayed by the details! Good, you're engaging with her arguments.

options.text: Mesmo assim, estou a precisar de um fim de semana mais calmo. Fica para a próxima.
options.translation: Even so, I need a calmer weekend. Maybe next time.
options.feedback: Polite refusal. It's good to know how to decline an invitation.

nodes.npc_text: Percebo perfeitamente. Às vezes precisamos de paz. Então, fica para a próxima. Mas se mudares de ideias, é só dizer!
nodes.npc_translation: I understand perfectly. Sometimes we need peace. So, next time then. But if you change your mind, just say so!

options.text: Obrigada, Maria! Bom arraial para ti. Até breve!
options.translation: Thanks, Maria! Have a good festival. See you soon!
options.feedback: You ended the conversation politely, wishing her well. Well done.
